given_idea: Using a pseudo-parallel corpus generated by an unsupervised machine translation model facilitates the structural similarity of the two embedding spaces and improves the quality of cross-lingual word embeddings in the unsupervised mapping method.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: A fully unsupervised framework for learning multilingual word embeddings will substantially outperform previous approaches in multilingual word translation and cross-lingual word similarity.

Unsupervised bilingual word embeddings jointly trained on synthetic parallel data significantly outperform unsupervised mapped bilingual word embeddings in several cross-lingual natural language processing tasks.

An unsupervised method that automatically builds the monolingual parallel corpus for text simplification using sentence similarity based on word embeddings will outperform existing methods that require human annotation.
An unsupervised system for parallel corpus filtering will improve the quality of machine translation systems for low-resource languages.
Training unsupervised neural machine translation with unsupervised bilingual word embedding agreement will significantly improve its performance compared to conventional methods.

The proposed unsupervised multilingual embedding method performs well under resource-poor scenarios, significantly outperforms existing unsupervised and supervised methods trained with bilingual pairs of words.
An unsupervised learning approach will optimize transformation functions between monolingual word embedding spaces without requiring cross-lingual labeled data.
The cross-lingual pre-training method incorporating explicit cross-lingual training signals will significantly improve the performance of unsupervised machine translation.
The performance of unsupervised bilingual word embedding and cross-lingual masked language model has a significant influence on the performance of unsupervised neural machine translation.
Incorporating word and subword units in unsupervised machine translation will improve the fluency and consistency of translations.
Resource-lean cross-lingual embedding models may outperform resource-intensive models using full-blown machine translation in some settings.
Using word embeddings for word alignment will outperform previous word embeddings-based methods in segment-level machine translation evaluation.

The proposed method outperforms off-the-shelf bilingual embeddings on the task of unsupervised cross-language part-of-speech tagging and on the task of semi-supervised cross-language super sense tagging.
The discriminative word alignment model integrated into a Transformer-based machine translation model will achieve major improvements over unsupervised baselines in cross-lingual word alignment.
Aligning monolingual word embedding spaces in an unsupervised way can build a bilingual dictionary between two languages without using any parallel corpora.
Extracting parallel sentences from comparable corpora significantly improves the performance of statistical machine translation systems.

The combination of unsupervised neural and statistical machine translation systems will achieve superior performance in the German-to-Czech translation task compared to other language pairs under low-resource conditions.
Filtering the pseudo-parallel corpus using quality estimation based on sentence-level round-trip translation will improve machine translation performance for low-resource language pairs.

The robust self-learning method for fully unsupervised cross-lingual mappings of word embeddings succeeds in all tested scenarios and obtains the best published results in standard datasets, even surpassing previous supervised systems.

Cross-lingual named entity recognition model transfer without the use of parallel corpora will outperform the strong machine translation baseline when transferring the English model to Spanish and Chinese.
The generative Bayesian model will achieve substantial performance gains in unsupervised constituency parsing over the CCM model when applied to bilingual parallel corpora.
The reproduction of the robust self-learning method for fully unsupervised cross-lingual mappings of word embeddings is feasible with some minor assumptions.
Obtaining large-scale pseudo parallel corpora by back-translating monolingual data will significantly improve the translation accuracy of context-aware neural machine translation models.

High quality crosslingual word embeddings can be built for many languages in a unified vector space, enabling high performance on bilingual lexicon induction, monolingual similarity, and crosslingual document classification tasks.
Improving statistical machine translation for resource-poor languages by exploiting their similarity to resource-rich ones will yield significant gains in translation accuracy.

An unsupervised learning algorithm for identification of paraphrases from a corpus of multiple English translations of the same source text will yield phrasal and single word lexical paraphrases as well as syntactic paraphrases.

Multilingual unsupervised neural machine translation scheme jointly trains multiple languages with a shared encoder and multiple decoders, outperforming separately trained bilingual models by up to 1.48 BLEU points.
The proposed method obtains state of the art results on the task of unsupervised word translation while requiring less computational resources than competing approaches.

A deep generative model of machine translation which incorporates a chain of latent variables will improve translation performance by accounting for local lexical and syntactic variation in parallel corpora.
Multilingual unsupervised neural machine translation framework will significantly improve translation quality by more than 3 BLEU score on six benchmark unsupervised translation directions.
Using comparable corpora will improve the accuracy and coverage of statistical machine translation models trained on low amounts of parallel data.

A new robust framework for learning unsupervised multilingual word embeddings will mitigate instability issues and yield results on par with state-of-the-art methods in bilingual lexicon induction, while outperforming supervised baselines in multilingual document classification and multilingual dependency parsing.
The unsupervised speech-to-text translation system achieves comparable BLEU scores to supervised end-to-end models despite the lack of supervision.
Neighborhood sensitive maps for multilingual word embeddings (NORMA) outperforms current state-of-the-art methods for word translation between distant languages.

Adapting unsupervised machine translation methods for automatic style transfer will outperform previous state-of-the-art models in terms of accuracy of style transfer and quality of input-output correspondence.
Methods to learn splitting rules from monolingual and parallel corpora will improve the performance of statistical machine translation systems.
The first unsupervised text simplification system based on phrase-based machine translation will outperform some supervised baselines on the WikiLarge and WikiSmall benchmarks.
Cross-lingual supervision improves unsupervised neural machine translation by utilizing supervision signals from high resource language pairs to enhance the translation of zero-source languages.

The transfer learning method using crosslingual word embeddings in a sequence-to-sequence model achieves state-of-the-art accuracy of 85.7% for English and improves performance for German compared with several baseline domain adaptation techniques.
Iterative Normalization consistently improves word translation accuracy of three cross-lingual word embedding methods.

Neural machine translation context vectors serve as an effective interlingua representation for parallel sentence identification, achieving an F1 score of 98.9% when combined with similarity measures.
Learning cross-lingual word embeddings improves language models when textual training data is limited to as few as a thousand sentences.
Translated texts yield better language models for statistical machine translation than original texts.
A novel semi-supervised method to learn cross-lingual word embeddings for bilingual lexicon induction will outperform existing models by a good margin.
An_unsupervised_adaptation_method_will_improve_neural_machine_translation_performance_by_fine_tuning_a_pre_trained_out_of_domain_model_using_a_pseudo_in_domain_corpus.
The proposed approach for cross-lingual relation extraction model transfer based on bilingual word embedding mapping achieves very good performance for a number of target languages.
Cross-lingual joint entity and word embedding learned from cross-lingual data can disambiguate linkable entities and effectively represent unlinkable entities.
The random-walk-based approach to learning paraphrases from bilingual parallel corpora outperforms the state-of-the-art system of Callison-Burch (2008).
Using pre-trained embeddings as features in a regression model will improve sentence-level quality estimation of machine translation.
Cross-lingual pretraining will improve the performance of language models on cross-lingual classification and machine translation tasks.

given_idea: The lack of semantic alignment between translations and the inability to penalize low-quality literal translations hinders cross-lingual encoders' ability to evaluate machine translation quality.
original: Cross-lingual encoders perform poorly as semantic encoders for reference-free machine translation evaluation due to a semantic mismatch between representations of mutual translations and the inability to punish low-quality literal translations.

Employing semantic embeddings to round-trip translation-based quality estimation metric will achieve higher correlations with human judgments compared to existing metrics.
Resource-lean cross-lingual embedding models may outperform resource-intensive models using full-blown machine translation in some settings.
Tuning a machine translation system against a semantic frame based objective function produces more robustly adequate translations than tuning against BLEU or TER.
Cross-language aligned word embeddings as the sole source of semantic information mirror human error annotation in neural machine translation.
Unsupervised methods in neural machine translation fail to produce reasonable translations due to linguistic dissimilarity and domain mismatch between source and target monolingual data.
Current state-of-the-art methods perform rather poorly on the FLORES evaluation datasets for low-resource machine translation.
Different alignment models will impact the translation quality of a statistical machine translation system.
The ranking of machine translation systems strongly correlates with human judgments of translation quality.
Detecting content-heavy sentences in cross-lingual context will improve the understandability of translations for English speakers.
Adapting translation models to translationese improves statistical machine translation quality.
Document-level context is not interpretable as utilizing the context in neural machine translation.

Source-target domain mismatch affects training of machine translation systems on low resource languages.
The sensitivity of neural machine translation architectures to common hyperparameters significantly impacts translation quality.
Online decoding constraints impact translation quality in neural machine translation architectures.
Automatic metrics correlate with human judgments of translation quality.

Automatic metrics correlate with human judgments of translation quality for evaluation metrics.
Segment-level and strong machine translation systems pose big challenges for automatic metrics evaluation.
Choosing a non-English pivot language improves translation quality for certain language pairs in statistical machine translation.
Encoder-free models have different effects on alignment quality for German → English and Chinese → English.
An automatic evaluation metric based on rank correlation coefficients modified with precision will outperform conventional metrics for evaluating translation quality between distant language pairs.
Modeling translationese and original text as separate languages in a multilingual model can improve the naturalness of machine translation outputs.
Contextual embeddings improve machine translation evaluation metrics, achieving higher correlation with human judgments than existing metrics.
Training neural machine translation systems on naively mixed data can degrade performance versus models fit to each constituent domain.
Current Machine Translation systems fail to render the lexical diversity of human-generated or translated text.
The exploration of six challenges for neural machine translation reveals deficiencies and improvements over the quality of phrase-based statistical machine translation.
Existing context-aware models do not improve discourse-related translations consistently across languages and phenomena.
Inadequate temporal processing, in the form of poor encoder representations, is a bottleneck for both Productivity and Systematicity in Neural Machine Translation.

Fully unsupervised cross-lingual word embedding methods fail to induce meaningful cross-lingual word embeddings for a large number of language pairs and never surpass the performance of weakly supervised methods.

The performance of evaluation metrics varies significantly across different levels of Machine Translation quality, with metrics performing poorly when faced with low-quality translations and not capturing nuanced quality distinctions.
The use of human references as ground truth for cross-language word embeddings is not proper unless one does not understand how native speakers process semantics in their cognition.
Filtering the pseudo-parallel corpus using quality estimation based on sentence-level round-trip translation will improve machine translation performance for low-resource language pairs.
Using pre-trained embeddings as features in a regression model will improve sentence-level quality estimation of machine translation.
Using word embeddings for word alignment will outperform previous word embeddings-based methods in segment-level machine translation evaluation.
Translated texts yield better language models for statistical machine translation than original texts.
Subjective human judgements can be effectively replaced with an automatic annotation procedure in Machine Translation Quality Estimation.
Comparing word embeddings obtained from massive and curated corpora will reveal that the quality of data significantly impacts the performance of semantic representations for low-resourced languages.
Semantic annotations provide a high degree of stability across translations compared to syntactic annotations in structure-aware Machine Translation systems.
Assigning sentence-level quality scores for very noisy corpora will enable the sub-selection of high-quality data for training machine translation systems.
Enforcing the one translation per discourse constraint in Statistical Machine Translation can potentially improve translation quality.
Recovering empty categories with a structured MaxEnt model improves word alignment quality and enhances performance in a large-scale state-of-the-art syntactic machine translation system.
A multilingual approach using target-language embeddings will improve end-to-end spoken language translation performance under data scarcity conditions.
Translating classification models with highly incomplete bilingual dictionaries will improve performance in cross-lingual text classification.

The robustness of sentence encoding approaches for neural machine translation encoder-decoder modeling will be better represented under the neural machine translation framework compared to phrase-based machine translation when translating utterances containing automatic speech recognition errors.

given_idea: TransESC will generate more smooth and effective supportive responses compared to previous methods in Emotion Support Conversation.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: The Emotional Support Conversation (ESC) task and the ESC Framework will enhance the ability of dialog systems to provide effective emotional support.

Positive emotion elicitation in chat-based dialogue systems will result in dialogue responses that are more natural and elicit a more positive emotional response.
The emotion cause transition graph will produce more empathetic, coherent, informative, and specific responses than existing models.
The model that adds the emotion vector in the decoder will produce better outcomes in dialogue generation compared to other models.

The proposed model obtains significantly more favorable results than the state-of-the-art model in dialogue emotion recognition.

The proposed approach to contentful neural conversation results in more contentful responses compared to previous methods, improving both informativeness and diversity of generated output.
A Hierarchical Transformer with Speaker Modeling will achieve better performance in Emotion Recognition in Conversation compared to existing methods.
DialogBERT will generate more coherent, informative, and human-like responses compared to existing models such as BART and DialoGPT.
Feedback-Aware Double Controlling Network (FADO) will achieve state-of-the-art performance in terms of both strategy selection and response generation for Emotional Support Conversation.
Empathetic response generation with explicit Self-Other Awareness (EmpSOA) will produce more empathetic responses compared to previous methods.
Self-talk models produce better-quality responses compared to models that directly generate responses given a dialogue history.

Recognizing the emotion cause improves empathetic response generation in conversations.

The two-stage conversational agent for emotional dialogue generation will outperform comparison models in emotion generation while maintaining semantic performance.

Transformers can successfully leverage linguistic information to improve their valence predictions in speech emotion recognition.

Partner, a deep reinforcement learning agent, will successfully generate more empathic, specific, and diverse responses compared to existing NLP methods for empathic dialogue generation.
TransferTransfo will show strong improvements over current state-of-the-art end-to-end conversational models in terms of perplexity, Hits@1, and F1 metrics.
A new annotation scheme allows the selection of two verbal labels per segment to better represent blended emotions in customer interactions.
Aspect-guided response generation will provide more interactive and informative responses for better communication between the agent and the user.
ProsocialDialog will enable conversational agents to generate more socially acceptable dialogues compared to existing state-of-the-art language and dialogue models.
Global-to-Local Hierarchical Graph Network will capture multi-source information and model hierarchical relationships, leading to improved performance in emotional support conversations.
The emotional dialogue system (EmoDS) can generate meaningful responses with a coherent structure while expressing the desired emotion explicitly or implicitly within a unified framework.
The Emotion-Reinforced models will generate stories that follow the desired emotion arcs significantly better compared to baseline methods, without sacrificing story quality.
User emotion look-ahead is key to modeling and generating empathetic dialogue responses.

The Dial2Desc system will generate more accurate and descriptive results using a new neural attentive model that exploits the interaction between utterances from different speakers compared with other baselines.

given_idea: An in-context learning-based named entity recognition approach can effectively inject in-context NER ability into pre-trained language models and recognize entities of novel types on-the-fly using only a few demonstrative instances.

A deep heterogeneous graph alignment network over the video shots and question words will improve the integration of inter- and intra-modality information for video question answering.
The multilevel model that integrates vision and language features will significantly outperform prior work on text-based activity retrieval in video.
The hierarchical spatio-temporal attention network will effectively learn the joint representation of dynamic video contents for open-ended video question answering.
The method for visual question answering will outperform the long short-term memory-based approach in answering complex questions.
Structured Two-stream Attention network will significantly outperform existing methods in video question answering tasks.
TVQA is a large-scale video question answering dataset that requires systems to jointly localize relevant moments, comprehend subtitle-based dialogue, and recognize relevant visual concepts.
Question-Guided Spatio-Temporal Contextual Attention Network (QueST) will achieve superior performance in video question answering compared to existing methods.
Deep Embedded Memory Networks will outperform other question-answering models in video story question-answering tasks.
The model will obtain state-of-the-art visual question answering performance by simultaneously generating question-relevant captions.
ActivityNet-QA dataset will improve video question answering performance compared to existing VideoQA baselines.
The proposed approach can predict the form of the answer from the question in Visual Question Answering.
A new model that explicitly reasons about different temporal segments in a video will improve the localization of moments in video based on temporal language queries.
Spatio-Temporal Answerer with Grounded Evidence (STAGE) will effectively ground evidence in both spatial and temporal domains to answer questions about videos.

The novel approach to developing a high-performing visual question answering system can elucidate its answers with integrated textual and visual explanations that faithfully reflect important aspects of its underlying reasoning.
The graph-based approach for Visual Question Answering will achieve state of the art results and demonstrate interpretability.
Applying adversarial techniques during inference will improve the fluency, coherence, and relevance of multi-sentence video descriptions.

The method for visual question answering combines an internal representation of the content of an image with information extracted from a general knowledge base to answer a broad range of image-based questions.
A joint model that integrates visual, audio, and textual features can be effectively used to identify sentiment in Web videos.
The combined Visual and Textual Question Answering model will significantly improve visual question answering performance over a strong baseline model by utilizing paragraph captions.
A multi-strategy and multisource question answering system can effectively answer natural language questions.
The co-attention model for Visual Question Answering will improve performance by jointly reasoning about image and question attention in a hierarchical fashion.
The LSTM-based model enables utterances to capture contextual information from their surroundings in the same video, thus aiding the classification process.
The method learns representations for word meanings from short video clips paired with sentences.
Maximizing mutual information between the image, the expected answer, and the generated question results in more diverse, goal-driven questions.
Neural module networks will achieve state-of-the-art results on visual question answering tasks by exploiting the compositional structure of questions.
The knowledge-enabled visual question answering model will outperform traditional visual question answering and knowledge base-based question-answering methods on the text-KVQA dataset.
Generating commonsense descriptions from videos will enhance video captioning by incorporating intentions, attributes, and effects.
Balancing the Visual Question Answering dataset by collecting complementary images will lead to a more accurate assessment of state-of-the-art Visual Question Answering models.
Neural module networks will achieve state-of-the-art results in visual question answering by exploiting the compositional linguistic structure of questions.
The method learns to answer visual questions by selecting image regions relevant to the text-based query.
The image-question-answer synergistic network will improve the performance of visual dialog systems by valuing the role of the answer.
Sharing knowledge with temporally-directed unsupervised video prediction and logically-directed language entailment generation will improve video captioning performance.
UniViLM: a Unified Video and Language pre-training model for both multimodal understanding and generation will achieve state-of-the-art results on five downstream tasks.
Models that effectively combine both query-question and query-answer representations using attention mechanisms in a hierarchical manner yield the best results for FAQ Retrieval.

The model sequentially selects from detected objects and learns interactions between objects that influence subsequent selections, outperforming the state of the art architecture for visual question answering on multiple metrics that evaluate counting.

Linking instance segmentations provided by COCO to the questions and answers in the Visual Question Answering dataset will significantly benefit both individual tasks and unified vision and language modeling.
Models must utilize all the available inputs (video, audio, question, and dialog history) to perform best on this dataset.
New evaluation schemes will compensate for over-represented question-types in visual question answering algorithms.
The proposed Relation-VQA (R-VQA) framework will achieve state-of-the-art performance in Visual Question Answering by effectively utilizing visual relation facts.
The proposed approach successfully takes into account both the local and global temporal structure of videos to produce descriptions.
A simple bag-of-words baseline for visual question answering will show comparable performance to recent approaches using recurrent neural networks.
Decomposing a language query into relevant and irrelevant components will improve the localization of video moments described by natural language queries.
Rephrasing visual questions by controlling the ambiguity of the questions improves the visual question answering process.

given_idea: Using Bregman divergence provides a unified interpretation of softmax cross-entropy and negative sampling loss functions, allowing for a fair comparison of the two.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Weighted maximum likelihood loss optimizes the expected F-measure of maximum entropy classifiers.
The generalized large-margin softmax loss (L-Softmax) explicitly encourages intra-class compactness and inter-class separability between learned features.
Any f-divergence can be used for training generative neural samplers.
Fenchel-Young losses constructed from a generalized entropy will yield losses with a separation margin and probability distributions with sparse support.
Bregman divergence provides a rich framework to estimate unnormalized statistical models for continuous or discrete random variables.
A convex formulation for positive and unlabeled classification can cancel bias while using different loss functions for positive and unlabeled samples.
Cosine loss function provides substantially better performance than cross-entropy on datasets with only a handful of samples per class.
Generalized cross entropy loss functions will yield good performance in a wide range of noisy label scenarios compared to mean absolute error and categorical cross entropy loss.
Smooth loss functions designed for top-k classification will outperform cross-entropy loss in terms of robustness to noise and overfitting.
Maximizing the soft nearest neighbor loss in the hidden layers leads to improved generalization and better-calibrated estimates of uncertainty on outlier data.
Minimizing the reverse cross-entropy will improve the detection of adversarial examples in deep learning models.
Models trained with lattice-free maximum mutual information provide a relative word error rate reduction of ∼ 11.5% over those trained with cross-entropy objective function.
Energy scores better distinguish in- and out-of-distribution samples than the traditional approach using the softmax scores.
Mirror descent induced by Bregman divergence proximity functions is equivalent to the natural gradient descent algorithm on the dual Riemannian manifold.
Minimum entropy regularization enables the incorporation of unlabeled data in standard supervised learning and provides robustness to the violation of the 'cluster assumption'.
The general evaluation framework for generative models measures the trade-off between precision and recall using Renyi divergences.
The divergence associated with smooth, proper, and convex loss functions is upper bounded by the Kullback-Leibler divergence.
Penalizing low entropy output distributions acts as a strong regularizer in supervised learning.
Families of Alpha-, Beta- and Gamma-divergences exhibit consistent properties and can be generated from one another through suitable nonlinear transformations.
Using dice loss in replacement of the standard cross-entropy objective will significantly improve performance on data-imbalanced NLP tasks.
A unified bias-variance decomposition will provide insights into the relationships between various loss functions and the trade-offs between bias and variance in classification.
Regularizing the posterior difference with f-divergence can result in well-improved model robustness.

A general framework for learning low-variance, unbiased gradient estimators for black-box functions of random variables will improve the optimization process in deep learning and reinforcement learning.
Aligned cross entropy (AXE) substantially improves performance on major WMT benchmarks for non-autoregressive models.

Algorithms for distributionally robust optimization of convex losses with conditional value at risk and chi-squared divergence uncertainty sets require a number of gradient evaluations independent of training set size and number of parameters.
The log-Spherical Softmax and log-Taylor Softmax outperform the log-softmax loss on MNIST and CIFAR-10 tasks.
Circle loss offers a more flexible optimization approach towards a more definite convergence target compared with the loss functions optimizing (s_n-s_p).
Using Maximum Mutual Information as the objective function in neural models will produce more diverse, interesting, and appropriate responses compared to traditional objective functions.
The expressiveness of Softmax-based models is limited by a Softmax bottleneck, and the proposed method improves the state-of-the-art perplexities on Penn Treebank and WikiText-2.
Persistent Contrastive Divergence algorithm outperforms standard Contrastive Divergence and Pseudo-Likelihood algorithms in modeling and classifying various types of data.
Wasserstein elliptical embeddings are more intuitive and yield tools that are better behaved numerically than Gaussian embeddings with Kullback-Leibler divergence.

The Jensen-Shannon divergence-based evaluation method achieves comparable performance with the common automatic evaluation method ROUGE in single documents summarization task and better performance than ROUGE in multiple document summarization task.
Minimizing the prediction divergence between the two complementary networks can lead to significant performance gains in semi-supervised learning.
The systematic approaches developed in this paper will yield f-divergence inequalities that provide tight bounds on the relative entropy and Bhattacharyya distance in terms of χ2 divergences.
The instance based softmax embedding method achieves significantly faster learning speed and higher accuracy than all existing methods.

A continuum of lower bounds that encompasses previous bounds and flexibly trades off bias and variance will improve the estimation and representation learning of Mutual Information in high-dimensional problems.
The α-Bridge will unify the advantages of maximum likelihood and adversarial learning, enabling the smooth transfer from one to the other via the α-divergence.
Using contrastive loss function will improve the discriminative learning of features in speech emotion recognition compared to the cross-entropy loss.

The variational approximation and the variational upper bound methods will perform better in approximating the Kullback Leibler divergence between Gaussian mixture models compared to existing methods.
Skip-Gram with Negative Sampling captures second-order co-occurrence information similarly to Singular Value Decomposition, while Pointwise Mutual Information is agnostic to it.
The novel algorithm for learning fair representations leads to a better utility-fairness trade-off on balanced datasets compared with existing algorithms.
The unbiased gradient estimator designed for importance-sampled objectives is both simpler and more effective than the NVIL estimator proposed for the single-sample variational objective.

A generalized entropy regularized policy optimization formulation can unify various sequence prediction algorithms, demonstrating that they are special instances of the framework with differences in reward function configurations and hyperparameters.
A simple and effective unified encoder will outperform the baseline models of dual-encoder models in terms of BLEU and METEOR scores.
Hard negative samples improve downstream performance in contrastive learning.
Entropy-SGD will improve generalization over stochastic gradient descent in training deep neural networks.
Variable-kernel similarity metric (VSM) learning will show better generalization than backpropagation and most other learning methods on standard classification data sets.
Semi-analytical approximations of moments of Gaussian random variables passed through sigmoid or softmax mappings yield at most 5% error.
Proximal methods based on entropy-like distances will achieve global convergence results for the minimization of convex functions subject to nonnegativity constraints.
The proposed multiset loss function is more effective than various baseline loss functions for multiset prediction.

given_idea: BERM will improve the generalization ability of dense retrieval by capturing matching signals through semantic unit balance and essential matching unit extractability.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Multiple representation dense retrieval can be enhanced using pseudo-relevance feedback, leading to improved effectiveness in reranking and dense retrieval operations.
The lexicon-enlightened dense retriever can bring consistent and significant improvements in large-scale retrieval performance compared to existing dense retrieval models.
Dense retrieval significantly outperforms sparse retrieval that uses object expansion.
Unifier unifies dense-vector and lexicon-based retrieval in one model with a dual-representing capability.
This paper presents the first thorough examination of the zero-shot capability of dense retrieval models.
Dense representations provide valuable relevance signals, improving BM25 results in sparse–dense hybrids.
Isotropic representation can improve the performance of dense retrieval models.
Pre-training large bi-encoder models on domain-matched tasks will significantly improve performance in information retrieval compared to existing methods.
The Unified Semantic Matching framework will achieve state-of-the-art performance in information extraction tasks and demonstrate strong generalization ability in zero/few-shot transfer settings.
Building semantic models for the first-stage retrieval can achieve high recall efficiently.
RoDR, a novel robust dense retrieval model, significantly improves the retrieval results on both the original queries and different types of query variations.
Improving dense retrieval performance under low-resource scenarios is crucial for choosing the right technique.
SimLM will show substantial improvements over strong baselines in dense passage retrieval tasks.
Generating pseudo query embeddings for dense retrieval will improve document representations and enhance retrieval performance compared to traditional sparse vector space models.
Combining dense knowledge-based features and sparse word translations will yield considerable improvements in cross-language retrieval compared to existing methods.
The multi-hop dense retrieval approach achieves state-of-the-art performance on multi-hop datasets without requiring corpus-specific information.

Bi-Granular Document Representation will effectively handle massive-scale embedding-based retrieval with strong advantages in accuracy, achieving significant recall gains on million-scale and billion-scale corpora.
CLEAR will complement lexical retrieval with semantic embedding retrieval, improving retrieval effectiveness compared to classic bag-of-words and BERT-enhanced models.
Fine-tuned dense retrieval models significantly outperform other systems in statutory article retrieval.
ANCE-PRF will significantly outperform ANCE and other recent dense retrieval systems on several datasets.
Learning-based approaches have great potential to further improve the retrieval performance.
Bi-encoder models are highly complementary to existing structure search methods and advance the state-of-the-art on Math Information Retrieval datasets.
The proposed framework using retrieval methods will enhance both the pre-training and fine-tuning for commonsense generation.
Momentum adversarial Domain Invariant Representation learning (MoDIR) will improve the generalization ability of dense retrieval models in zero-shot settings compared to existing methods.
The Dual Retrieval Module will enhance the performance of semi-supervised relation extraction by jointly optimizing the retrieval and prediction tasks.
The new models significantly outperform other semantic models in retrieval performance.
Dense phrase retrieval can serve as the basis for coarse-level retrieval including passages and documents.
SparTerm will significantly improve the representation capacity of bag-of-words method for semantic-level matching while maintaining efficiency, interpretability, and exact term matching.

The new retrieval system facilitated by pretrained language model (i.e., Enhanced Representation through kNowledge IntEgration) can largely improve the usability and applicability of our search engine.
The retrieval model with additional data significantly outperforms retrieval models directly trained with gold annotations on Precision at N and Mean Reciprocal Rank.
Improving information retrieval using neural retrievers in the biomedical domain will lead to large gains over existing neural approaches and outperform BM25 in the small-corpus setting.
Deep learning will significantly improve the performance of content-based image retrieval systems by effectively bridging the semantic gap.
Benchmarking-IR (BEIR) will facilitate the evaluation of information retrieval models and improve understanding of their out-of-distribution generalization capabilities.
Query2doc improves both sparse and dense retrieval systems by expanding queries with pseudo-documents generated by large language models.
MatchPyramid models can significantly outperform several recently introduced deep matching models on the retrieval task.
The proposed approach enables better generalization, achieving state-of-the-art performance in text-to-image and image-to-text retrieval tasks, and caption-caption similarity tasks.
Dense lexical representations (DLRs) can effectively approximate the original lexical representations, preserving effectiveness while improving query latency.
Hierarchical semantic retrieval at both paragraph and sentence level is vital for effectively filtering upstream information and shaping upstream data distribution for downstream modeling.
Conversational Dense Retrieval system, ConvDR, will outperform previous systems that operate in the sparse word space in terms of retrieval accuracy and efficiency.
Multi-modal retrieval based framework (MoRe) will improve the performance of named entity recognition (NER) and relation extraction (RE) by integrating textual and visual cues.
Language Model-based solutions perform better in semantic retrieval from legal texts, especially when unsupervised fine-tuning is applied.

Learning To Retrieve (LTRe) training technique significantly outperforms all competitive sparse and dense baselines in terms of effectiveness and provides more than 170x speed-up in the training process compared to the previous state-of-the-art Dense Retrieval method.
Models finetuned solely on our synthetic datasets outperform strong baselines such as BM25 as well as recently proposed self-supervised dense retrieval methods.
The joint training approach for dense passage retrieval and passage re-ranking will lead to mutual improvement in performance.
DrBoost produces representations which are 4x more compact while delivering comparable retrieval results compared to standard dense retrieval models.
Mixture-Of-Memory Augmentation (MoMA) will improve the zero-shot generalization ability of language models in dense retrieval tasks.
The deep relevance matching model (DRMM) can significantly outperform existing retrieval models and state-of-the-art deep matching models for ad-hoc retrieval tasks.
Document Augmentation for dense Retrieval (DAR) significantly outperforms relevant baselines on the dense retrieval of both the labeled and unlabeled documents.
Leveraging large-scale hyperlinks and anchor texts to pre-train the language model for ad-hoc retrieval will lead to more accurate and reliable pre-training samples compared to existing methods.
Rethinking with retrieval (RR) can produce more faithful explanations and improve the performance of large language models (LLMs) on complex reasoning tasks.

given_idea: While neural language models excel in many areas, they still fall short when it comes to generating idioms, crafting engaging stories, and recognizing subtle patterns in literature.
original: Neural language models struggle on tasks involving proverb recommendation, narrative generation, and motif identification compared to humans.

top_k.sentence: Pre-trained language models fail in effectively capturing the meanings of multiword expressions, especially idioms, and fine-tuning could provide a sample efficient method of learning representations of sentences containing multiword expressions.
Most current neural network models are not able to generalize well in the task of natural language inference.
Current neural network models are not able to generalize in capturing the semantics of natural language inference, but seem to be overfitting to the specific dataset.

Despite the recent successes of large language models on tasks aimed to assess commonsense knowledge, these models do not greatly outperform simple word-level models based on pre-trained word embeddings.
Neural language models struggle on tasks involving proverb recommendation, narrative generation, and motif identification compared to humans.
Massively pretrained language models, such as OpenAI GPT2-117, do not consistently generate better quality text compared to state-of-the-art neural story generation models.
Contemporary Natural Language Processing systems are fairly successful models of human word similarity, but they fall short in many other respects.
Pre-trained language models can identify analogies to a certain extent, but struggle with abstract and complex relations.
Two recently proposed neural models that perform well on formal texts are limited in their performance when applied to social media data.
Pretrained language models capture a significant amount of information about scalar magnitudes but are short of the capability required for general common-sense reasoning.

Neural models generalize well to unseen words in tests on five languages but provide no clear benefit over the naïve baseline for downstream part-of-speech tagging of an English historical collection.
Contemporary standard natural language processing models are limited in their compositional generalization capacity.
Neural language models trained on linguistic data can suppress and recover expectations while predicting words, but do not fully recover their previous grammatical state.

Current natural language inference models do not perform consistently well on transitivity inference tasks, suggesting that they lack the generalization capacity for drawing composite inferences from provided training examples.
Pretrained models can learn to represent linguistic features with little pretraining data, but require far more data to learn to prefer linguistic generalizations over surface ones.

Existing models trained on language modeling and translation produce strong representations for syntactic phenomena, but only offer comparably small improvements on semantic tasks over a non-contextual baseline.
Current deep learning methods are not sufficiently sample efficient for learning a language with compositional properties.
Generative Adversarial Networks (GANs) for language generation do not perform convincingly better than conventional Language Models (LM).
Long contexts, but not their detailed syntactic and propositional content, are important for the low perplexity of current transformer language models.
Language modeling is an effective pretraining task for sentence understanding, but the paradigm of pretraining and freezing sentence encoders may not be ideal for further work.
Language models are more successful at learning grammatical constraints than they are at learning truly referential information.
Neural word prediction models can distinguish grammatical from ungrammatical sentences with high accuracy, but multilingual models generally underperform monolingual models.
Deep learning methods are not as competitive in language identification related tasks as they are in many other text classification tasks.
Current state-of-the-art natural language processing models still leave much room for improvement in learning alignments between machine and human preferences.
Increasing the size of the network and the corpus on which it is trained yields diminishing returns in improving syntactic prediction accuracy in neural language models.
Current state-of-the-art natural language understanding systems do not generalize systematically, despite overall high performance.
Current natural language understanding benchmarks fail to meet essential criteria for effective evaluation, and adversarial data collection does not address the underlying issues.
Neural language representations still only learn associations that are explicitly written down.
Designing a symbolic intermediate representation will reduce the frequency of failed outputs in neural natural language generation systems.
Refining language models can generate coherent and grammatically sound sentences that explicate implicit knowledge connecting sentence pairs in texts.
Phrase representation in deep transformer models relies heavily on word content, with little evidence of nuanced composition.
Augmenting deep learning models with linguistically motivated features does not improve state-of-the-art performance.

Exposing language models to a related language does not always increase grammatical knowledge in the target language, and optimal conditions for lexical-semantic transfer may not be optimal for syntactic transfer.
Powerful contextualized word representations deal less well with the subtle in-context similarity relationships needed for substitution compared to models trained with this objective in mind.
High-performance neural language models are sensitive to input perturbations and their performance can decrease even when small changes are introduced.
Generative linguistics is unlikely to fulfill its promise of accounting for language learning if it continues to maintain its distance from neural and statistical approaches to learning.
A neural model that generalizes better by representing paraphrases in a continuous space will improve performance on both the noun-compound paraphrasing and classification tasks.
TuringAdvice reveals that language understanding models struggle to generate helpful advice in natural language, with the best model achieving human-level performance only 14% of the time.
LSTM-based language models can achieve similar results in Word Sense Disambiguation with much less data than previously suggested.

The commonsense ability of GPT, BERT, XLNet, and RoBERTa is promoted by language modeling objectives, bi-directional context, and larger training sets, but current models perform poorly on tasks requiring more inference steps.
Current neural machine translation systems are good at translating pronouns with intra-sentential reference, but inter-sentential cases remain difficult.
The representations learned by the InferSent model fail to capture the needed compositionality.

Neural architectures used for language modeling exhibit different inductive biases, with LSTMs showing little preference for word ordering and transformers displaying a clear preference for certain orderings.
State-of-the-art natural language inference systems exhibit limited generalization ability, failing to capture many simple inferences.
Modeling a passage as a unified dense vector is not optimal; a refined model based on contextual sentences will alleviate internal representation conflicts.
BERT does not fully live up to the natural expectations for a semantic vector space.
Incorporating syntactic structure into neural language models will improve language modeling performance as evidenced by lower perplexity and better quality parse trees.
Deep networks are capable of subtle grammar-dependent generalizations, but do not rely on systematic compositional rules.
Models based on our hypotheses perform better at capturing words while generating stories in the target persona.

Training a recurrent neural network (RNN) on a large corpus of written text aligned to its normalized spoken form will not be sufficient to solve the text normalization problem without additional methods.

given_idea: When sustainability initiatives prioritize individual actions over systemic changes, they may encounter consumer resistance, yet fostering a sense of ownership can still motivate responsible behavior when accompanied by clear accountability and transparent decision-making processes.
original: The unsupervised FrameNet-based embeddings model (CM-Lex) is competitive with recent deep learning metaphor generation systems, and the controlled seq-to-seq generation model (CM-BART) outperforms all other models in both automatic and human evaluations.
Incorporating sememe knowledge into semantic compositionality models will significantly improve their performance compared to baseline methods that do not consider sememe knowledge.

top_k.sentence: Libertarian paternalism can influence behavior while respecting freedom of choice.
The behavioral influence strategy predicated on self-perception theory will produce persistent behavior change.
Incorporating the Sustainable Development Goals into corporate strategy and corporate social responsibility projects will enhance value creation for society, economy, and environment in Kenya.
The application of the Fairness, Accountability, and Transparency framework to the proposed algorithm will significantly improve its ethical adherence and outcomes.
There is a growing awareness in the field concerning ethical issues, which translates into a willingness to get involved in ethics-related actions.
Mindset research illustrates how beliefs can influence human behavior and that an era-bridging program of research can continue to be generative and relevant.
Personality variables and processes influence people's efforts to manage their public images.

An external intervention should stay close to the potential exiter's own doubt, make the influence attempt as subtle as possible, use narratives and self-affirmatory strategies to reduce resistance to persuasion, and consider the possibility to promote attitudinal change via behavioral change as an alternative to seek to influence beliefs directly.
Emotions are often reliable reflections of moral beliefs and are open to conscious regulatory control, enabling the individual to use emotional responses to serve moral goals.
Participatory experience is a mindset and an attitude about people that requires new ways of thinking, feeling, and working.
Consumer preferences are exercised on characteristics of goods rather than the goods themselves.
Ethical and logistical planning is essential to successful community-centred knowledge mobilization.
Specially structured labor to maintain efforts' forward motion can help collective action publics take action.
Values motivate behavior, but the relation between values and behaviors is partly obscured by norms.
The tailoring-ingredients influence persuasion through different psychological processes.
Proactive system behaviour is perceived trustworthy in insecure situations independent of the timing.
The broader impact statement can be implemented in future iterations to better align with potential goals.
Ethical outcomes ought to be achieved by design, i.e. by following a process aligned by ethical values.
Motivational interviewing provides practitioners with skills to address resistance to change in individuals with addiction disorders.
Moral rationalization arises from a conflict of motivations and a need to see the self as moral, leading to a violation of moral principles.
Different types of behavior can be interpreted in a single unifying formalism - as a reward-rational choice that the human is making, often implicitly.
Individual rationality is a function of social norms.
This article contributes a purposeful approach to thematic analysis in order to systematize and increase the traceability and verification of the analysis.

Past behavior guides future responses through two processes: well-practiced behaviors recur due to automatic processing, and conscious decision making is necessary for behaviors not well learned or performed in unstable contexts.

The positive outcome frame will be more effective than the negative outcome frame in motivating actual: ought subjects to change their eating patterns; the opposite will be true for actual: ideal subjects.
Distributive justice beliefs are influenced by whether one considers the primary utility or value of their society to be well-being or power.
The Observer Effect causes data to behave cyclically: rising and falling as momentum shifts for businesses.
Understanding the cultural bases for ethical behavior in both the USA and China can arm a marketer with knowledge needed to succeed in cross‐cultural business.
Positive stereotypes may contribute to and perpetuate systemic differences in power and privilege.
A consumer-oriented, stage-based model of psychological recovery focuses on intrapersonal processes.
Cleanliness of the servicescape has a strong impact on customers’ responses in multiple service contexts.
The way new and existing users engage with a community depends strongly and systematically on the nature of the collective identity it fosters.
Consumers follow four paths to trust in smart technology: one path relates to the perceived personality of the technology’s voice interface and three non-anthropomorphism-based trust paths.
Counselor adherence to Motivational Interviewing principles is associated with more productive within-session client behavior in a smoking cessation trial for African American smokers.
Witnessing a moral deed influences individuals to perform moral deeds themselves and allows them to act immorally.
Guilt arousal is positively related to donation intention, and persuasion and agent knowledge impact the extent of guilt aroused.
The natural environment is an easily identifiable primary stakeholder when climate change is brought into the debate.

Affect control models predict that actors are motivated to behave in ways that are consistent with self-meanings, maintain cultural norms about the suitability of behaviors and emotions to role relations, and account for behavior and emotion in prior interactions.
Social-contextual factors nurture intrinsic motivation and promote internalization, leading to desired educational outcomes.
The proposed foraging based measure provides a more accurate reflection of the utility and of observed behaviours compared to common measures.

The explanation interface can be more effective to increase users' product knowledge, preference certainty, perceived information usefulness, recommendation transparency and quality, and purchase intention.
Concrete intervention strategies can counter implicit biases for key players in the justice system.
Moral framing has inconsistent effects on charitable donation behavior, despite positive associations with donation sentiment and motivation.

The behavior model (FBM) asserts that for a person to perform a target behavior, he or she must be sufficiently motivated, have the ability to perform the behavior, and be triggered to perform the behavior.
Behavioral activation (BA) has been investigated over time and its emerging application to a broad range of populations and problems.
A definition of trust and a model of its antecedents and outcomes are presented, which integrate research from multiple disciplines and differentiate trust from similar constructs.
Causal reasoning is necessary for creating fair algorithms for decision making.
Incremental self-improvement will enable multi-agent reinforcement learning systems to adapt effectively in changing environments.
Many experimental manipulations may actually be manipulating goal activation.
The proposed method is able to work in evolving environments.

given_idea: The method applying deep metric learning will achieve substantially better performance than existing methods for argument clustering in semantic frame induction.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Deep metric learning will significantly improve the performance of semantic frame induction using contextualized embeddings.

The semantic frame induction method using masked word embeddings and two-step clustering will effectively reduce reliance on surface information of frame-evoking verbs and improve the clustering of instances of the same verb.
Unsupervised Lexical Frame Induction will effectively cluster verbs and their arguments to resemble semantic frame structures.

Using a graph clustering algorithm on contextualized embedding representations of verbs and arguments will outperform existing baselines in terms of Purity F1 and BCubed F1 for unsupervised lexical semantic frame induction.
Neural methods based on a recently released language model will significantly outperform earlier methods for argument-pair classification.
A deep learning architecture will considerably improve upon existing techniques that use syntactic features and supervised classifiers for relation-based argument mining.

A combined approach that employs dense representations from hidden layers of a masked language model and sparse representations based on substitutes for the target word in the context will improve semantic frame induction.
An unsupervised algorithm for identifying verb arguments will achieve higher precision compared to existing supervised algorithms.
The method for semantic role labeling will achieve strong results on standard PropBank and FrameNet corpora through joint embeddings of arguments and semantic roles.
The proposed approach discovers dramatically more accurate clusters than models without sense disambiguation.
Employing topic models will yield better results in constructing semantic classes from raw semantic classes compared to alternative approaches.
Contextualized word embeddings will improve the classification and clustering of topic-dependent arguments compared to traditional methods.
The system for semantic frame induction showed the best performance in Subtask B.1 and finished as the runner-up in Subtask A of the SemEval 2019 Task 2 on unsupervised semantic frame induction.
The semi-supervised model outperforms previous models of frame prediction and improves performance with unlabeled training data.
The novel learning method for word embeddings significantly outperforms a baseline based on a previously introduced word embedding method for semantic relation classification.

Modeling the sequence of arguments in social media posts using neural models with embeddings for words, discourse relations, and semantic frames will significantly improve the detection of successful arguments.
Classifying argumentative relations based on four logical mechanisms will significantly improve classification performance compared to unsupervised baselines.

The proposed model for Event Argument Extraction will exploit both syntactic and semantic structures of sentences to learn more effective sentence structures, leading to state-of-the-art performance on standard datasets.
The proposed technique for learning semantic representations will outperform the prior state of the art in cross-lingual document classification tasks.
The proposed domain alignment learns a semantically meaningful representation which outperforms previous work.

The proposed computational model for argument mining in online persuasive discussion forums will achieve significant improvements compared to recent state-of-the-art approaches using pointer networks and a pre-trained language model.
Enriching, axiomatizing, and cleaning up frame relations will improve the conceptual organization of FrameNet for knowledge-intensive natural language processing tasks.
The resulting embeddings capture semantic similarity better than regular distributional methods and other dictionary-based methods.
The graph-based semi-supervised learning framework will achieve substantial improvement in semantic parsing compared to the base system that does not utilize the learned lexicon.
The neural semantic parsing system will learn new high-level abstractions through decomposition more efficiently and reliably than existing methods.
Learning from labeled semantic parses significantly improves overall performance, resulting in absolute 5 point gain compared to learning from answers.
A method that integrates compositional semantics into learning performs better than all other alternatives.
Novel denotational similarity metrics will be at least as beneficial as distributional similarities for tasks that require semantic inference.
The proposed models improve the performance on metaphor interpretation and generation, and the learned representations also benefit nominal metaphor detection compared with strong baselines.

Identifying causality in verb-noun pairs by exploiting deeper semantics of nouns and verbs will achieve around 15% improvement in F-score over a supervised classifier trained using linguistic features.
An unsupervised method will detect the stance of argumentative claims with respect to a topic, outperforming BERT.
Using named entity linking for argument identification boosts performance over the traditional approach, and there is further improvement from using argument types.

Natural Language Generation techniques should be used to build practical working systems.
The proposed model is beneficial for multi-modal summarization and superior to existing methods.
Exploiting descriptive feedback will enable the hierarchical phrase-based captioning model to perform better than when given independently written human captions.

Integrating knowledge into pretrained language models will improve their knowledge awareness and performance on natural language processing tasks.

given_idea: By utilizing the MIMIC-RRS dataset, researchers can develop and evaluate radiology report summarization models that are capable of adapting to diverse anatomies and modalities, thereby improving their overall effectiveness and factual correctness.
original: The proposed dataset (MIMIC-RRS) will enable better evaluation of radiology report summarization models across multiple anatomies and modalities.

Optimizing a neural summarization model with a factual correctness reward via reinforcement learning will substantially improve the factual correctness and overall quality of outputs for radiology summaries.

top_k.sentence: The proposed dataset (MIMIC-RRS) will enable better evaluation of radiology report summarization models across multiple anatomies and modalities.
A unified framework for exploiting both extra knowledge and the original findings in an integrated way will facilitate impression generation in radiology reports.
Incorporating content planning in a summarization model yields output of higher quality and allows the creation of synthetic datasets resembling real-world document-summary pairs.
MIMIC-CXR is a large dataset of chest radiographs that facilitates research in computer vision, natural language processing, and clinical data mining.
Reinforced cross-modal alignment for radiology report generation will improve the quality of generated reports by better aligning visual and textual features.
Automating the generation of radiology impressions with neural sequence-to-sequence learning will significantly improve the efficiency and accuracy of summarizing radiology findings.

Introducing new rewards to encourage the generation of factually complete and consistent radiology reports will significantly improve the performance of image-to-text radiology report generation systems.

Optimizing a neural summarization model with a factual correctness reward via reinforcement learning will substantially improve the factual correctness and overall quality of outputs for radiology summaries.

The RadGraph reward method will improve the factual completeness and correctness of generated radiology reports compared to existing methods.
The proposed model is beneficial for multi-modal summarization and superior to existing methods.
The method will outperform similarity-based methods on datasets in a range of domains including news, medical journal articles, and personal anecdotes.

An end-to-end evaluation framework for expansion-based interactive summarization will improve the customization and comparison of summary results.
A new approach to human evaluation will improve the consistency and transparency of multi-document summarisation in the biomedical literature.
Contrastive Attention model will improve the performance of existing models in chest X-ray report generation by better capturing and describing abnormal regions.
New approaches that capitalize on domain-specific models will modestly improve the factual accuracy of generated summaries.
The proposed method is effective and surpasses state-of-the-art systems on the standard summarization dataset.

A sequence-to-sequence abstractive summarization model augmented with domain-specific ontological information will significantly outperform the current state-of-the-art on the task of clinical report summarization in terms of rouge scores.
The Posterior-and-Prior Knowledge Exploring-and-Distilling approach (PPKED) will outperform previous state-of-the-art models in generating radiology reports on MIMIC-CXR and IU-Xray datasets.
Using existing summaries as soft templates will improve the stability and readability of generated summaries in seq2seq summarization systems.
Sequence-to-sequence modeling is a promising approach for automatic generation of medical reports in the presence of data at scale.
The new dataset for joint Text Simplification and Summarization is well suited to simplification.
Scientific papers are suitable for data-driven text summarization.

The radiology report generation model utilizing the transformer architecture produces superior reports as measured by both standard language generation and clinical coherence metrics compared to competitive baselines.
Pretrained models are better summarizers in generating faithful and factual summaries as evaluated by humans.
WikiHow dataset will provide a diverse and high-quality resource for training abstractive summarization models.
The task of hospital-course summarization will improve the generation of concise yet comprehensive narratives from clinical documentation.

A data-driven approach for performing automated quality assessment of radiologic interpretations will achieve high precision and recall in matching radiologic interpretations with pathology diagnoses.
The proposed model demonstrates superiority in multi-modal supplementary and complementary summarization compared to existing techniques.
The new approach for creating hierarchical summarization corpora will enhance the development and evaluation of hierarchical summarization systems.
Using Abstract Meaning Representation (AMR) as a form of content representation will improve the feasibility of generating abstracts from multi-document summarization.
Data-derived hypotheses may help to explain what types of reframes are helpful for users.
The generative encoder-decoder model will achieve state-of-the-art performance in automatic radiology report generation compared with other baseline approaches.

top_k.sentence: BBTv2 can achieve comparable performance to full model tuning and state-of-the-art parameter-efficient methods under few-shot settings while maintaining much fewer tunable parameters.

Efficient methods can provide insight into the relationship between hyperparameter settings and performance in machine learning.
Robust Dynamic Inference Networks (RDI-Nets) can achieve better accuracy and robustness with over 30% computational savings compared to the defended original models.
The joint reranking-and-verification model achieves performance comparable to the closed-domain state-of-the-art on the TabFact dataset.

RadGraph Benchmark achieves a micro F1 of 0.82 and 0.73 on relation extraction on the MIMIC-CXR and CheXpert test sets respectively.

Instance-Dependent Prompt Generation (IDPG) will consistently outperform various prompt tuning baselines and be on par with other efficient transfer learning methods while tuning far fewer model parameters.

The ME-CNER framework will achieve a large performance improvement on the Weibo dataset and comparable performance on the MSRA news dataset with lower computational cost against existing state-of-the-art alternatives.
The parameter-efficient Mixture-of-Experts architecture will improve performance and efficiency of pre-trained language models while reducing total parameters compared to existing models.
Torch-Struct will provide significant performance gains over fast baselines in structured prediction tasks.

The dynamic graph interactive framework with label-semantic injection (DGIF) significantly outperforms existing approaches, obtaining a relative improvement of 6.5% over the previous best model on the MixATIS dataset in overall accuracy.
Compacter will achieve a better trade-off between task performance and the number of trainable parameters compared to prior parameter-efficient fine-tuning methods.

The framework for evaluating inference-rule resources simplifies the instance-based evaluation method, producing a large amount of annotations with high inter-annotator agreement for a low cost and short period of time.

ABCD achieves comparable performance as two parsing baselines on MinWiki and higher accuracy on the number of atomic sentences than an encoder-decoder baseline on DeSSE.
The HIT model will achieve state-of-the-art performance in nested named entity recognition compared to existing methods.

given_idea: The neuro-symbolic approach combining interpretable rules based on first-order logic with neural learning will achieve competitive or better performance in short-text entity linking compared to state-of-the-art black-box neural approaches.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: DeepType outperforms all existing solutions for Entity Linking by integrating symbolic information into the reasoning process of a neural network.

Combining multi-step inference of symbolic logical reasoning with the generalization capabilities of neural networks will improve reasoning about entities and relations in text and large-scale knowledge bases.

The neural network model jointly learns distributed representations of texts and knowledge base entities and achieves state-of-the-art results on sentence textual similarity, entity linking, and factoid question answering.
Combining deep neural networks with structured logic rules will enhance the performance of neural networks in tasks such as sentiment analysis and named entity recognition.
A neural, end-to-end model for jointly extracting entities and their relations will match or exceed state-of-the-art performance without relying on external natural language processing tools.
Neural entity linking systems developed since 2015 outperform classic methods on common benchmarks.

The symmetrical attention-based neural network with entity structural features will improve the performance of entity linking in knowledge graph construction, achieving an average F1 score of up to 0.866.
The neuro-symbolic model learns entire rule systems from a small set of examples and outperforms neural meta-learning techniques in three domains.
The neural network approach will yield state-of-the-art performances for entity disambiguation compared to existing methods.
The Neural Collective Entity Linking model (NCEL) will improve linking performance and generalization ability by integrating local contextual features and global coherence information.
The neural entity linking model will yield state-of-the-art results in English and cross-lingual tasks by effectively utilizing multi-lingual embeddings.

Integrating logical knowledge in the form of first-order logic into a deep learning system will enhance neural outputs with knowledge regularization via logic rules.

The proposed end-to-end neural network with recurrent random-walk layers for collective entity linking will achieve better performance than other state-of-the-art models.
The neural-symbolic reasoner will outperform state-of-the-art models in link prediction on Commonsense Knowledge Graphs.
Integrated neural-symbolic systems can effectively process symbolic knowledge within artificial neural networks.
Joint learning of named entity recognition and entity linking improves performance in both tasks compared to models trained with individual objectives.
A neural network model that learns to discriminate between felicitous and infelicitous arguments for a particular predicate will achieve state of the art performance on a pseudo-disambiguation task.
The neural model that reads two sentences to determine entailment using long short-term memory units will outperform previous models and classifiers in recognizing entailment relations.
The neural framework developed in this study will effectively empower the sequence labeling task by extracting knowledge from raw texts without relying on additional supervision.
A neural-based approach to named entity recognition for social media texts that incorporates both local and augmented semantics will demonstrate superiority over previous studies.
LinkNBed, a deep relational learning framework, will demonstrate substantial improvements in link prediction and entity linkage over state-of-the-art relational learning approaches.
The novel framework provides key properties of both neural networks and symbolic logic, yielding a highly interpretable disentangled representation.

The path-constrained random walk model learns syntactic-semantic inference rules for binary relations and shows significant accuracy improvements in binary relation prediction over methods that consider only text or only the existing knowledge base.
Neural-symbolic computing integrates neural learning with symbolic knowledge representation and reasoning to construct explainable AI systems.

A lightweight neural method for biomedical entity linking will require fewer parameters and computing resources while remaining competitive with previous BERT-based methods on standard evaluation benchmarks.
Neural Entity Reasoner will outperform conventional Named Entity Recognition models in most cases on both English and Chinese NER datasets.
Probabilistic Soft Logic improves results in Semantic Textual Similarity compared to Markov Logic Networks and a purely distributional approach.

The approach using a Graph Neural Network architecture combined with a decoder copy mechanism generates logical forms with entities that are competitive with state-of-the-art methods and outperforms existing approaches when combined with BERT pre-training.
The novel notion of semantic relatedness between two entities represented as sets of weighted keyphrases improves the quality of prior link-based models for entity disambiguation.

An expressive neural tensor network suitable for reasoning over relationships between two entities will improve performance when entities are represented as an average of their constituting word vectors.
Generating natural language statements that can be logically entailed by the facts in an open-domain semi-structured table will improve logical inference in neural natural language generation models.
Exploiting Neural Networks in Meaning Representation parsing will improve the performance of the transition-based approach.
A unified, readily scalable neural approach reconciles all subtasks like intent prediction and knowledge retrieval, outperforming commercial systems relying on complex rules.
Entity Linking using Densified Knowledge Graphs (ELDEN) improves entity linking performance by utilizing densified knowledge graphs.
The neural network model with a variant of cross-entropy loss function and hierarchical loss normalization will outperform existing methods for Fine-grained Entity Type Classification.
Neural Attentive Bag-of-Entities model will achieve state-of-the-art results in text classification using entities in a knowledge base.

The neural graphical model for parsing natural language sentences into their logical representations will achieve new state-of-the-art results on standard benchmark datasets across different languages.
A simple and efficient neural architecture for cross-lingual named entity recognition will achieve competitive performance with the state-of-the-art.

The proposed end-to-end neural network architecture can selectively learn important procedure-specific relationships among sentences and outperforms existing entity relationship extraction algorithms.
Self-Governing Neural Networks (SGNNs) are effective at capturing low-dimensional semantic text representations while maintaining high accuracy.
Incorporating segment information into neural relation extraction will improve the performance of relation extraction models.
Tree-structured recursive neural networks can learn suitable representations for logical inference in natural language.
Integrating logic rules and neural models will improve the accuracy and consistency of responses to comparison questions in question answering tasks.
The unified neural architecture for semantic role labeling and relation classification will significantly improve performance on both tasks compared to state-of-the-art approaches.
