Fine-tuning BERT on semi-formal steps that explain how math results are derived will improve its mathematical abilities.
The Edge-Enhanced Hierarchical Graph-to-Tree model (EEH-G2T) can effectively improve performance in math word problem solving compared with state-of-the-art methods.

The reverse operation based data augmentation method will produce new high-quality math problems that improve the performance of state-of-the-art math word problem solving models.

given_idea: The success of sustainability efforts depends on striking a balance between individual and collective responsibility, as excessive focus on either one can lead to consumer resistance or diffused ownership, ultimately undermining the effectiveness of the intervention.
original: Transfer learning-based approaches perform better than task-specific models trained on 3 times as much data.
SERENGETI outperforms other models on 11 datasets across the eight tasks, achieving 82.27 average F_1.

top_k.sentence: Tech worker collective action can serve as a countervailing force against corporate impulses that prioritize profit over other values.
Moral rationalization arises from a conflict of motivations and a need to see the self as moral, leading to a violation of moral principles.
The way new and existing users engage with a community depends strongly and systematically on the nature of the collective identity it fosters.
Libertarian paternalism can influence behavior while respecting freedom of choice.
Digital infrastructure empowers protest movements in specific ways, but some of the same mechanisms of digitally-fueled empowerment have paradoxically led to disempowering side effects.
Social norms evolve in ways that depend upon the psychological dispositions of the individual and may impair social efficiency.
Consumer preferences are exercised on characteristics of goods rather than the goods themselves.
Power and stereotyping are mutually reinforcing, mediated by attention.
Real life explanatory processes are often constrained by multiple goals, epistemic and directional, where these goals may mutually reinforce one another or may conflict.
Communities, policy-makers, and technologists need to work intimately together to lift up each other's goals.
Orienting crowd work around high-level goals may enable workers to coordinate their effort to accomplish complex work.
The Observer Effect causes data to behave cyclically: rising and falling as momentum shifts for businesses.
The essential tension between productive tradition and risky innovation affects a scientist’s choice of research problem.
Whether depletion takes place or not depends on a person’s belief about whether willpower is a limited resource.
Distributive justice beliefs are influenced by whether one considers the primary utility or value of their society to be well-being or power.
The effectiveness of a group's control over the members of the group increases monotonically with increase in the autonomy of the group.

People often mis-interpret the explanations, and superficial and unrelated factors, such as word length, influence the explainees’ importance assignment despite the explanation communicating importance directly.
Specially structured labor to maintain efforts' forward motion can help collective action publics take action.
Positive stereotypes may contribute to and perpetuate systemic differences in power and privilege.
Values motivate behavior, but the relation between values and behaviors is partly obscured by norms.
Personality variables and processes influence people's efforts to manage their public images.

Focusing participants on either the descriptive norms or the injunctive norms regarding littering caused the participants' littering decisions to change only in accord with the dictates of the then more salient type of norm.

Popularity bias is more likely to hinder quality below and above a critical exploration cost, but an optimal balance exists where choosing what is popular can help promote high-quality items to the top.
Individual rationality is a function of social norms.
Political spectrum polarization depends on three conditions: attitude to private property, individual freedom, and wealth redistribution.
Any increase in institutional utility necessarily leads to a corresponding increase in social burden.
Titles with a contradictory slant trigger more people to identify bias compared to titles with a miscued slant, yet visualizations are perceived as impartial by the majority.
A combination of multiple mitigations may make an important difference in preventing the threat of AI-enabled influence operations.
Misinformation undermines the process of acquiring and sharing reliable information, impacting critical areas such as climate change, democratic decisions, and pandemic control.
Community moderation of toxic behavior happens at a higher rate than previously estimated.

The graph-based recurrent retrieval approach will achieve state-of-the-art results in multi-hop open-domain question answering.

Knowledge-aided open-domain question answering (KAQA) method improves relevant document retrieval and candidate answer reranking by considering the relationship between a question and the documents and the relationship between candidate documents.
Retrieval Question-Answering (ReQA) will serve as a benchmark for evaluating large-scale sentence-level answer retrieval models.
The verbalizer-retriever-reader framework for open-domain question answering can effectively benefit from the expanded knowledge index, leading to large gains over text-only baselines.
Extending existing strong question answering frameworks to include previous questions will improve the overall question-answering accuracy in open-domain question answering.
The joint passage retrieval model (JPR) achieves significantly better answer coverage on three multi-answer datasets compared to prior approaches.
The open-domain question answering approach using web tables significantly outperforms state-of-the-art baseline approaches for both factoid and non-factoid queries.

RE^3QA, a unified question answering model that combines context retrieving, reading comprehension, and answer reranking, will outperform the pipelined baseline and achieve state-of-the-art results on two versions of TriviaQA and two variants of SQuAD.

Combining state of the art language models with abductive information retrieval, information gain based re-ranking, passage selection, and weighted scoring will achieve a 72.0% accuracy, an 11.6% improvement over the current state of the art in open book question answering.
The multi-step reasoning framework brings consistent improvement when applied to two widely used reader architectures DrQA and BiDAF on various large open-domain datasets.
The method for retrieving multiple supporting paragraphs will achieve state-of-the-art performance over the SQuAD-Open and HotpotQA datasets for multi-hop open-domain question answering.
Using Probably Asked Questions (PAQ), the RePAQ QA-pair retriever can match the accuracy of recent retrieve-and-read models while being significantly faster.
Reinforced Ranker-Reader (R3) significantly improves on the state of the art for multiple open-domain question answering datasets.
SPARTA achieves new state-of-the-art results across a variety of open-domain question answering tasks in both English and Chinese datasets.
Generative models for open domain question answering can benefit from retrieving text passages, potentially containing evidence.
Cross-lingual Open-Retrieval Answer Generation (CORA) substantially outperforms the previous state of the art on multilingual open QA benchmarks across 26 languages.

A linguistically-based approach to answering non-factoid open-domain questions from unstructured data will achieve near-to-human-performance results and be competitive with state-of-the-art systems on SQuAD and MS-MARCO datasets.
The multi-domain question rewriting dataset improves question quality by an average of 45 points across three aspects and achieves a 13.2% improvement in BLEU-4 over baseline methods.
The R2-D2 pipeline will surpass state-of-the-art performance on open-domain question answering datasets.
NEQA will improve answering quality over time and adapt to new domains in open-domain question answering over knowledge bases.
The probabilistic answer selection framework will effectively boost the ranking of the correct answer by using multiple resources and considering evidence of similarity among answer candidates.

Ranking paragraphs and aggregating answers using Paragraph Ranker improves performance of open-domain question answering pipeline on the four open-domain question answering datasets by 7.8% on average.

Retrieval-Augmented Language Model pre-training (REALM) outperforms all previous methods for Open-domain Question Answering by a significant margin (4-16% absolute accuracy) while providing interpretability and modularity.
AnswerFact will improve the prediction of answer veracity in product question answering platforms compared to existing methods.
The architecture developed for open-domain question answering will significantly improve the ability to find relevant excerpts from large text collections compared to traditional indexing methods.

Dense Hierarchical Retrieval (DHR) significantly outperforms the original dense passage retriever and helps an end-to-end Question Answering system outperform the strong baselines on multiple open-domain Question Answering benchmarks.
The semantic parsing framework based on semantic similarity will achieve higher precision and improve F1 by 7 points in open-domain question answering tasks compared to previous approaches.
Structural relationships between questions and their candidate answer passages can significantly improve the accuracy of answer re-ranking models in question answering systems.
Binary Passage Retriever (BPR) substantially reduces the memory cost from 65GB to 2GB without a loss of accuracy on two standard open-domain question answering benchmarks.

A shared dense vector index coupled with a sequence-to-sequence model is a strong baseline, outperforming more tailor-made approaches for fact checking, open-domain question answering, and dialogue, and yielding competitive results on entity linking and slot filling.
PullNet improves over the prior state-of-the-art in open-domain question answering, particularly in settings where a corpus is used with an incomplete knowledge base.
The proposed method will achieve state-of-the-art performance for the sentence-level answer-selection task on the WikiQA and TREC-QA datasets.
Generating answers from a set of answer sentence selection candidates will improve accuracy in retrieval-based question answering systems.
The novel method for generating compact answers to open-domain why-questions will achieve significantly better ROUGE F-scores than existing encoder-decoder models and their variations.
ColBERT-QA improves OpenQA retrieval on Natural Questions, SQuAD, and TriviaQA, achieving state-of-the-art extractive OpenQA performance on all three datasets.
An additional in-domain pre-training stage using a large-scale, natural, and diverse question-answering dataset can be beneficial for open-domain question-answering.
The approach to non-factoid answer reranking achieves state-of-the-art performance on a public dataset of How questions, outperforming systems which employ sophisticated feature sets.
The proposed table cell search framework will outperform existing systems in retrieving answer cells from web tables for open-domain question answering.
Cluster-Former achieves state-of-the-art performance on several major question answering benchmarks.
The hybrid framework that takes both textual and tabular evidences as input will outperform baseline models that only take homogeneous input in open-domain question answering tasks.
The proposed framework supports the use of a combination of question-answer matching models and achieves 66% higher precision compared to IDF-weighted average of word vectors baseline.
Our approach will achieve near 4 percent improvement over the state-of-the-art in open-domain question answering task.

Open Predicate Query Language (OPQL) outperforms prior virtual knowledge base methods on two different knowledge base reasoning tasks and can be used as an external memory integrated into a language model leading to improvements on two open-domain question answering tasks.
The proposed approach for tackling multi-span questions based on sequence tagging leads to an absolute improvement of 29.7 Exact Match and 15.1 F1 compared to existing state-of-the-art results.

The proposed open-domain system with Paragraph Retriever and Paragraph Reader components will improve the performance of Question Answering systems by effectively retrieving relevant paragraphs and extracting answers using augmented linguistic features.

given_idea: The bi-encoder retriever for text-based dataset recommendation finds more relevant search results than existing third-party dataset search engines.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Pre-training large bi-encoder models on domain-matched tasks will significantly improve performance in information retrieval compared to existing methods.
Bi-encoder models are highly complementary to existing structure search methods and advance the state-of-the-art on Math Information Retrieval datasets.
Query expansion methods based on word embeddings significantly improve retrieval performance compared to using only the original query and also outperform the relevance model.
Tri-encoders with one encoder for each question, text, and table increase retrieval performance compared to bi-encoders with one encoder for the question and one for both text and tables.
Embedding-based retrieval will provide significant metrics gains in Facebook Search compared to traditional Boolean matching models.
The learned representations of research papers and datasets using heterogeneous variational graph autoencoder will yield more relevant dataset recommendations compared to naive retrieval methods.
The lexicon-enlightened dense retriever can bring consistent and significant improvements in large-scale retrieval performance compared to existing dense retrieval models.
Parameter selection heuristics achieve performance across word similarity and relevance datasets competitive with results previously reported in the literature.
The text-based recommendation engine utilizing recurrent neural networks will effectively map textual input into continuous vector representations tailored to the recommendation task.
Generating pseudo query embeddings for dense retrieval will improve document representations and enhance retrieval performance compared to traditional sparse vector space models.
Learning query-document similarities from a click-through bipartite graph with metadata will significantly improve relevance ranking and similar query finding compared to baseline methods.
Differentiable Search Index (DSI) significantly outperforms strong baselines such as dual encoder models in information retrieval.

Using Latent Dirichlet Allocation (LDA) for concept location in source code retrieval on StackOverflow will improve the precision and recall of code recommendations compared to traditional tf-idf methods.
Search agents, trained via behavioral cloning, outperform the underlying search system based on a combined dual encoder retriever and cross encoder reranker.
The Embedding and Mapping framework for Cross-Domain Recommendation (EMCDR) significantly outperforms state-of-the-art cross-domain recommendation methods.
Replacing document retrievers with large language model generators will improve performance on knowledge-intensive tasks.
DeepSentiBank significantly improves annotation accuracy and retrieval performance compared to its predecessors which mainly use binary SVM classification models.
The dual encoder model for entity retrieval outperforms discrete alias table and BM25 baselines and is competitive with the best comparable results on the TACKBP-2010 dataset.
Novel extensions to deep learning architectures will enhance the effectiveness of document and snippet retrieval tasks.
Retrieval Augmentation of queries provides Large Language Models with valuable additional context enabling improved understanding.
Large Language Models can deliver competitive, even superior results to state-of-the-art supervised methods on popular Information Retrieval benchmarks.
Uni-Retriever will unify knowledge distillation and contrastive learning to improve both high-relevance and high-CTR ad retrieval in Bing Sponsored Search.
Supplementing early-stage sampling with user-guided text search can effectively seed a classifier with positive documents without compromising generalization performance.

Bi-Granular Document Representation will effectively handle massive-scale embedding-based retrieval with strong advantages in accuracy, achieving significant recall gains on million-scale and billion-scale corpora.
Graph enhanced Entity Retrieval framework (GER) will capture more fine-grained information as complementary to sentence embeddings compared to existing methods.
An alternative model for description-based similarity significantly improves standard nearest neighbor search compared to current text embeddings.

The initial approach to the claim-relevance discovery problem leveraging various information retrieval and machine learning techniques achieves solid results on a large-scale dataset and outperforms state-of-the-art baselines.
The kNN-LM is most beneficial when retrieved items have high semantic similarity with the query.
div2vec improves the recommendation performance in terms of both accuracy and diversity.
The multi-vector model with textual guidance will improve performance on document similarity tasks in scientific datasets.
Deep Contextualized Term Weighting framework will improve the accuracy of first-stage retrieval algorithms compared to traditional term frequency methods.
Using positive and negative examples to search through the space of record matching queries will produce efficient and explainable queries for large datasets.
Integrating Pseudo Relevance Feedback with deep language models enhances the effectiveness of deep rerankers and dense retrievers.
The character-level architecture for learning bidirectional retrieval models will outperform state-of-the-art approaches in description and image retrieval tasks.
Learning-to-rank that accounts for query-dependent selection bias yields significant improvements in search effectiveness.
The retrieval model with additional data significantly outperforms retrieval models directly trained with gold annotations on Precision at N and Mean Reciprocal Rank.
Multiple representation dense retrieval can be enhanced using pseudo-relevance feedback, leading to improved effectiveness in reranking and dense retrieval operations.
The Dual Retrieval Module will enhance the performance of semi-supervised relation extraction by jointly optimizing the retrieval and prediction tasks.
RFMF, a pseudo-relevance feedback framework based on matrix factorization, significantly outperforms competitive baselines in improving retrieval performance.
The deep relevance matching model (DRMM) can significantly outperform existing retrieval models and state-of-the-art deep matching models for ad-hoc retrieval tasks.
Improving information retrieval using neural retrievers in the biomedical domain will lead to large gains over existing neural approaches and outperform BM25 in the small-corpus setting.
The introduced pseudo query vectors significantly outperform the average or sum of embedding vectors of all query terms method in information retrieval tasks.
Condenser improves over standard language models by large margins on various text retrieval and similarity tasks.
ANCE-PRF will significantly outperform ANCE and other recent dense retrieval systems on several datasets.
Leveraging both query-centric and passage-centric similarity relations will significantly improve dense passage retrieval performance compared to previous state-of-the-art models.
The adaptation of the vector-space model for ontology-based information retrieval will improve search over large document repositories compared to conventional keyword-based retrieval.
Querying language models by example significantly improves their performance on factual and commonsense knowledge retrieval.

Hypothetical Document Embeddings (HyDE) significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art unsupervised dense retriever Contriever and shows strong performance comparable to fine-tuned retrievers across various tasks.
The search models outperform a null model with the learning-based model outperforming the other approaches.
Expanding queries with synonyms generated from WordNet improves the precision and recall of code search results.

given_idea: Claim-Dissector is a novel latent variable model for fact-checking that achieves competitive results while using significantly fewer parameters compared to typical two-stage system pipelines.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: DISSECT is a toolkit to build and explore computational models of word, phrase and sentence meaning based on the principles of distributional semantics.
End-to-end multimodal fact-checking and explanation generation will improve the assessment of claim truthfulness compared to existing methods.
Detecting previously fact-checked claims can save time and effort in manual fact-checking processes.
Hierarchical Evidence Set Modeling (HESM) outperforms 7 state-of-the-art methods for fact extraction and claim verification.

This thesis presents research on automatic fact checking, including claim check-worthiness detection, stance detection, and veracity prediction, proposing more general machine learning solutions for natural language processing in the area of learning with limited labelled data.
A novel, unsupervised network-flow based approach will effectively discern true statements from false ones in computational fact checking.
A mixed-initiative approach to fact-checking improves participant accuracy in assessing claims when exposed to correct model predictions, but can degrade accuracy when the model is wrong.
ClaimBuster detects important factual claims in political discourses using natural language processing and supervised learning.
Automated fact checking research will benefit from unifying task formulations and methodologies across disciplines.
The ClaimBuster dataset can be leveraged in building computational methods to identify claims that are worth fact-checking.
Optimising both veracity prediction and explanation generation objectives simultaneously improves the performance of a fact checking system.
FactChecker will yield higher accuracy than existing approaches for truth-finding by leveraging linguistic features to infer believability of fact candidates.
Generating scientific claims from scientific sentences will improve zero-shot fact checking for biomedical claims.

An end-to-end model for evidence-aware credibility assessment of arbitrary textual claims will effectively aggregate signals from external evidence articles, improving the accuracy of misinformation detection.
Decision Tree-based Co-Attention model (DTCA) will provide explanations for the results of claim verification and achieve state-of-the-art performance in explainable claim verification.
Extracting the predicate of simple, manually-detected claims can generate novel claims effectively.
An automated claim verification solution encompassing document retrieval and veracity prediction will effectively mitigate COVID-19 misinformation.
The novel method for joint ranking of evidence pages and predicting veracity outperforms all baselines for automatic claim verification.

Incorporating model and data uncertainty estimates into natural language processing models for automatic rumour verification will improve the filtering of erroneous model predictions and enhance the prioritization of difficult instances for human fact-checkers.
DivDis will effectively learn a diverse collection of hypotheses from underspecified data and select one using minimal additional supervision.
A paragraph-level multi-task learning model for scientific fact-verification will improve the accuracy of verifying scientific claims compared to existing methods.

Duo-generative explainable misinformation detection (DGExplain) framework will significantly outperform state-of-the-art baselines in terms of the accuracy of multimodal COVID-19 misinformation detection and the explainability of detection explanations.
A multi-task learning approach will improve the performance of rumour verification compared to separate component training.
The unsupervised corpus-wide claim detection system outperforms previously reported results on the task of claim detection given relevant documents and labeled data.
The proposed two-stage distant supervision approach incorporating evidence into masked claims will achieve better results in factual error correction compared to existing methods.
The Dual-view model based on the views of Collective and Individual Cognition (CICD) will improve interpretable claim verification by capturing both global and local evidence while suppressing bias.
CredEye system for automatic credibility assessment will analyze the credibility of natural language claims by considering relevant articles from the Web.
Contextual features significantly impact the performance of automatic fact-checking systems.

Evidence-aware Hierarchical Interactive Attention Networks (EHIAN) will achieve state-of-the-art performance in explainable claim verification by capturing evidence fragments and fusing source credibility.
A two-staged sentence selection strategy will enhance evidence retrieval in the Fact Extraction and Verification challenge.
The latent variable model will achieve state-of-the-art results on semi-supervised natural language inference and paraphrase identification.
Using word embeddings to classify misinformation will enhance the efficiency of the fact-checking process by identifying similar content.
A general-purpose framework for fully-automatic fact checking using external sources will effectively distinguish false claims from factually true claims.
Automated fact-checkers suffer significant performance drops against synthetic adversarial evidence in both adversarial addition and adversarial modification settings.
The proposed data filtering method improves the model's performance and generalization in automatic fact-checking.
Jointly modeling sentence extraction and verification on the FEVER shared task will improve the accuracy of fact checking compared to separate models.
Learning to discriminate perturbations (DISP) can block adversarial attacks for text classification models without modifying the model structure or training procedure.

The proposed method exploits structured parsing information to detect claims without resorting to contextual information, achieving performance comparable to state-of-the-art methods that heavily rely on the context.
The hierarchical attention network will outperform state-of-the-art baselines in claim verification tasks.
The claim verification pipeline approach scored third in the shared task, out of 23 competing systems.
NewsClaims aims to benchmark claim detection systems in emerging scenarios, comprising unseen topics with little or no training data.
Existing fact checking systems that perform well on claims in formal style significantly degenerate on colloquial claims with the same semantics.
Deep latent variable models provide a rich, flexible framework for modeling many real-world phenomena.
A supervised learning approach based on a cascade of classifiers will effectively handle the skewed data inherent to the task of automatic claim detection in a given context.
The annotation schema and benchmark for automated claim detection will provide more consistent results across time, topics, and annotators compared to previous approaches.
This paper overcomes limitations by automatically assessing the credibility of emerging claims and generating suitable explanations from judiciously selected sources.
Utilizing the perplexity score in few-shot learning for fact-checking can outperform the Major Class baseline by more than an absolute 10% on the F1-Macro metric across multiple datasets.
Using graph convolutional network and graph attention network to propagate and aggregate information from neighboring nodes on the graph will improve the accuracy of fact checking.
The Enrichment Knowledge Distillation model will outperform existing methods in event detection, particularly for unseen and sparsely labeled trigger words.
Self-adaptive Mixture-of-Experts Network (SaMoE) will achieve better accuracy in table-based fact verification compared to previous state-of-the-art models.

given_idea: BITE is a backdoor attack that poisons the training data to establish strong correlations between the target label and a set of trigger words, demonstrating high attack success rate and stealthiness.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: A backdoor attack against LSTM-based text classification systems will achieve a success rate of around 96% with a 1% poisoning rate.
A backdoor attack targeting the BERT model will achieve a 100% attack success rate with a specific trigger while maintaining high accuracy on original samples.
A new data poisoning attack allows an adversary to control model predictions whenever a desired trigger phrase is present in the input.
Latent backdoors are a more powerful and stealthy variant of backdoor attacks that functions under transfer learning.

A backdoor poisoning attack utilizing conditional adversarially regularized autoencoder will successfully steer the predictions of a victim BERT finetuned classifier to the poison target class with success rates of >80% by adding just 1% poisoned data.
Adversarial and backdoor attacks based on text style transfer will achieve attack success rates exceeding 90% on popular NLP models.
Injecting backdoors into machine learning models based on poisoning the loss-value computation will demonstrate new classes of backdoors that are more powerful than those in prior literature.

Using syntactic structure as the trigger in textual backdoor attacks can achieve comparable attack performance to insertion-based methods while possessing much higher invisibility and stronger resistance to defenses.
The backdoor attack on text data achieves a 100% success rate with a sacrifice of 0.71% on text classification tasks.
Backdoors in deep learning models for source code can be installed using dataset poisoning, and they leave a spectral signature in the learned representation, enabling detection of poisoned data.
The novel form of backdoor attack can fool the model by pasting the trigger at random locations on unseen images, although the model performs well on clean data.

Backdoor injection attacks can achieve a high attack success rate (above 90%) with a small cost of model accuracy loss and a small injection rate, even under the weakest assumption wherein the adversary has no knowledge of the original training data or the classifier model.
The proposed weight-poisoning attack method introduces a layerwise weight poisoning strategy to plant deeper backdoors and a combinatorial trigger that cannot be easily detected.
Invisible backdoors activated by a learnable combination of word substitution can achieve nearly 100% attack success rate while remaining highly invisible to existing defense strategies.
Adding an extra training task to distinguish poisoned and clean data and using all the clean training data can significantly improve the performance of textual backdoor attacks.
A novel poisoning algorithm based on back-gradient optimization will effectively target a wider class of learning algorithms, including neural networks and deep learning architectures.
The proposed word-based backdoor attacking method will achieve greater stealthiness while maintaining good attacking performance compared to existing methods.
The layerwise weight poisoning strategy will enable the introduction of deeper backdoors in pre-trained models that are more difficult to detect.
A backdoored pre-trained model can behave maliciously in various downstream tasks without foreknowing task information.
Robustness-aware perturbations will effectively distinguish poisoned samples from clean samples to defend against backdoor attacks on natural language processing models.
TextAttack is a Python framework for adversarial attacks, data augmentation, and adversarial training in NLP.
Hidden backdoors can effectively fool modern language models and human inspection across three downstream security-critical NLP tasks with high attack success rates.
Label-consistent backdoor attacks can be executed efficiently by leveraging adversarial perturbations and generative models.

The decision-based attack strategy crafts high quality adversarial examples on text classification and entailment tasks, achieving a higher success rate with lower word perturbation percentage in a hard label black box setting.
SHARP, a new attack method, will perform a more potent attack compared with pioneer arts on structured prediction tasks.

The proposed methods for constructing triggers in natural language processing settings will achieve an almost perfect success rate for backdoor attacks without significantly affecting the original model utility.
BERT-Attack is a high-quality and effective method to generate adversarial samples using pre-trained masked language models exemplified by BERT.
BadPre is the first task-agnostic backdoor attack against pre-trained Natural Language Processing models.
Onion is a simple and effective textual backdoor defense based on outlier word detection that can handle all textual backdoor attack situations.
The prompt-based learning paradigm inherits universal vulnerability from the pre-training stage, where model predictions can be misled by inserting certain triggers into the text.
TextAttack is a library for running adversarial attacks against natural language processing models.

TextBugger is a general attack framework for generating adversarial texts that outperforms state-of-the-art attacks in terms of attack success rate, preserves the utility of benign text, and generates adversarial text with computational complexity sub-linear to the text length.
The novel training method can significantly reduce the attack success rate of models against backdoor attacks compared to undefended models.
Outsourced training introduces new security risks, allowing an adversary to create a backdoored neural network that performs well on training samples but behaves poorly on specific inputs.
BadNL is a general NLP backdoor attack framework that achieves an almost perfect attack success rate with a negligible effect on the original model’s utility.
A few-shot backdoor attack (FSBA) can implant hidden backdoors into visual object tracking models by tampering with the training process, significantly degrading their performance.
Outsourced training introduces new security risks, allowing adversaries to create backdoored neural networks that perform well on training data but behave poorly on specific inputs.

BadEncoder injects backdoors into a pre-trained image encoder such that the downstream classifiers built based on the backdoored image encoder for different downstream tasks simultaneously inherit the backdoor behavior.
Backdoor poisoning attacks can achieve an attack success rate of above 90% with only around 50 poisoning samples.
An attacker can launch an effective and efficient brute force attack that can craft instances of input to trigger each target class with high confidence in a target-agnostic manner.
An adversarial backdoor embedding algorithm can bypass existing detection algorithms including state-of-the-art techniques.
Decision-based membership inference attacks can achieve remarkable performance and can bypass most defense mechanisms against these attacks.
BERT is a lot more robust than research on attacks suggests.
Attacker Attribute Classification via Textual Analysis (AACTA) will reveal detailed information about attackers from adversarial texts.
An adversary can perform a training data extraction attack to recover individual training examples by querying the language model.
Variable-length textual adversarial attacks (VL-Attack) will effectively conduct adversarial attacks by integrating insertion, deletion, and replacement operations into a unified framework.
The proposed detection and mitigation system for deep neural network backdoor attacks will effectively identify backdoors and reconstruct possible triggers.

New inference attacks based on the intuition that inputs corresponding to training set members will be near a local minimum in the loss function can achieve high positive predictive value even in settings where other attacks are ineffective.
The novel optimization method for NLP backdoor inversion will effectively and efficiently detect and remove backdoors, outperforming 4 baseline methods.
Influence functions can provide valuable information for understanding model behavior, debugging models, detecting dataset errors, and creating visually-indistinguishable training-set attacks.

given_idea: In the context of sustainability, it is essential to recognize that individual-level changes are often insufficient to drive lasting impact, and that broader social transformations are necessary to create a culture of sustainability, which can be achieved by fostering a sense of collective ownership and responsibility for public goods.
original: The proposed multi-task pre-training strategy and new contrastive loss will significantly improve the effectiveness of new intent discovery compared to existing methods.
A multi-task trained model will outperform previous methods in the few-shot setting and rival specialized neural retrievers, even when in-domain training data is abundant.

Communities, policy-makers, and technologists need to work intimately together to lift up each other's goals.
Ethical and logistical planning is essential to successful community-centred knowledge mobilization.
The way new and existing users engage with a community depends strongly and systematically on the nature of the collective identity it fosters.

There is a need to interpret the use of Twitter data through the lens of social science research methods that imply a more reflexive ethical approach.
The concept of role as resource grants access to social, cultural, and material capital that incumbents and claimants exploit in order to pursue their interests.

Capabilities can help us to construct a normative conception of social justice, with critical potential for gender issues, only if we specify a definite set of capabilities as the most important ones to protect.
Values motivate behavior, but the relation between values and behaviors is partly obscured by norms.
Distributive justice beliefs are influenced by whether one considers the primary utility or value of their society to be well-being or power.

The proposed method is able to work in evolving environments.
People in different nations can differ in the behaviors that are seen as typical instantiations of values, while holding similar ideas about the abstract meaning of the values and their importance.
Social media data have properties quite different from those with which many social scientists are used to working, so the assumptions often used to plan and manage a project may no longer hold.
AI systems are not guaranteed to take decisions that are more objective than those of a human, but those systems can still be used to reduce social inequalities.
Personality variables and processes influence people's efforts to manage their public images.
The social meanings associated with released /t/ are constrained by linguistic and social factors.
The behavioral influence strategy predicated on self-perception theory will produce persistent behavior change.
This article contributes a purposeful approach to thematic analysis in order to systematize and increase the traceability and verification of the analysis.
There is a growing awareness in the field concerning ethical issues, which translates into a willingness to get involved in ethics-related actions.

The main wager of cultural psychology is that relatively few components of the human mental equipment are so inherently constrained, hard wired, or fundamental that their developmental pathway is fixed in advance and cannot be transformed or altered through cultural participation.
A distributed approach to harm mitigation that considers the entire life cycle of a dataset is necessary to address ethical concerns in dataset creation.
This review intends to present existing research on and practices employed in the assessment of societal impact in the form of a literature survey.

'This contribution attempts to sensitize researchers in the social and behavioral sciences to the importance of definitional issues and their consequences for distinguishing related but fundamentally different affective processes, states, and traits.'
Social-contextual factors nurture intrinsic motivation and promote internalization, leading to desired educational outcomes.
Misinformation undermines the process of acquiring and sharing reliable information, impacting critical areas such as climate change, democratic decisions, and pandemic control.
An expanded conceptualisation of recovery is proposed, in which difficulties are more prominently considered.
Moral rationalization arises from a conflict of motivations and a need to see the self as moral, leading to a violation of moral principles.
Clearly communicating need through the narrative is essential for the success of altruistic requests in online communities.

Basic notions of distributive justice are not universal intuitions of the human species but rather culturally constructed behavioral norms.
The reframing of normative femininity around neoliberal logics of private responsibility is one of neoliberalism’s central projects.
Postcolonial Theory might inform Participatory Design.
Speakers project different social identities and create different social relationships through their style choices.

Seven Grand Challenges are identified and presented in this article: Human-Technology Symbiosis; Human-Environment Interactions; Ethics, Privacy and Security; Well-being, Health and Eudaimonia; Accessibility and Universal Access; Learning and Creativity; and Social Organization and Democracy.

given_idea: Label agnostic pre-training will improve the ability of pre-trained language models to generalize to both seen and unseen data across varying aspects and domains in zero-shot text classification.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Zero-shot Learning approach for text categorization will generalize to unseen sentences, tags, and new datasets.
Zero-shot model adaptation to new tasks using natural language descriptions will achieve significant improvements in classification accuracy on benchmark text classification datasets.
Label Semantic Aware Pre-training (LSAP) will improve the generalization and data efficiency of text classification systems.
Developing few- and zero-shot methods for multi-label text classification will improve performance on infrequent labels in large label spaces.
Training pre-trained language models with pseudo-labeling data will improve the detection of counterfactuals in texts.

Using both types of pretrained language models for fully zero-shot learning of natural language understanding tasks will significantly outperform zero-shot prompting methods and achieve comparable results to strong few-shot approaches.

The Stanford Natural Language Inference corpus is prone to amplifying bias and stereotypical associations.
Models trained on edits encode different aspects of semantics and discourse than models trained on raw text.

given_idea: Seq2Seq-DU will outperform existing methods in dialogue state tracking across benchmark datasets.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Seq2Seq-DU will outperform existing methods in dialogue state tracking.
The semi-supervised explicit dialogue state tracker (SEDST) achieves comparable performance to state-of-the-art supervised learning baselines in state tracking procedure.
The context and schema fusion network will outperform strong baselines in multi-domain dialogue state tracking.
Global-Locally Self-Attentive Dialogue State Tracker (GLAD) significantly improves tracking of rare states compared to prior work.
Schema-guided multi-domain dialogue state tracker with graph attention networks will achieve state-of-the-art performance on MultiWOZ 2.0 and MultiWOZ 2.1 benchmarks.
Constructing a dialogue state graph will improve the performance of multi-domain Dialogue State Tracking compared to existing open-vocabulary approaches.

Dialogue State Tracking with Slot Connections model will significantly improve dialogue state tracking performance in multi-domain scenarios by explicitly considering slot correlations across different domains.
Dialogue State Tracker with Slot Attention and Slot Information Sharing (SAS) improves long dialogue context tracking by reducing redundant information's interference.
Fast Schema Guided Tracker will improve accuracy in dialogue state tracking while maintaining efficiency in computational and memory consumption compared to baseline models.
Formulating dialog state tracking as a semantic parsing task over hierarchical representations leads to a 20% improvement over state-of-the-art dialog state tracking approaches.
Zero-shot transfer learning with synthesized data can improve the accuracy of dialogue state tracking models.
BERT-DST outperforms prior work on benchmark scalable dialogue state tracking datasets Sim-M and Sim-R, and achieves competitive performance on the standard DSTC2 and WOZ 2.0 datasets.
StateNet, a universal dialogue state tracker, significantly outperforms the performance of state-of-the-art approaches.
The dialog state tracker combines a generative probabilistic model with a recurrent neural network for encoding dialog history, yielding competitive performance with top-performing trackers.

Graph-based and copy-augmented multi-domain dialogue state tracking will improve the performances of the multi-domain dialogue state tracking baseline with absolute joint accuracy improvements of 2.0% and 1.0% on the MultiWOZ 2.0 and 2.1 dialogue datasets, respectively.

A contextual hierarchical attention network with adaptive objective will enhance dialogue state tracking by effectively modeling interactions between slots and dialogue history while addressing the slot imbalance problem.
Fine-tuning BERT for schema-guided zero-shot dialogue state tracking will improve the model's ability to generalize on unseen service APIs.
A multi-task learning model with utterance tagging and a bidirectional language model will improve dialogue state generation performance on long context sequences.
Memory-to-sequence (Mem2Seq) can be trained faster and attain state-of-the-art performance on three different task-oriented dialog datasets.
The proposed scalable and accurate neural dialogue state tracking model reduces the latency in training and inference times by 35% on average, while preserving performance of belief state tracking.
A simple but effective dialog state tracking model based on BERT will outperform previous methods by a large margin, achieving new state-of-the-art results on the standard WoZ 2.0 dataset.
An end-to-end dialogue state tracking system will achieve better performance in predicting intents and slots compared to traditional stage-wise dialogue state tracking systems.
Using machine reading comprehension in dialogue state tracking will significantly improve accuracy in few-shot and zero-shot scenarios compared to existing approaches.
Modeling multi-domain dialogue state tracking as a question answering problem will improve generalization to unseen domains, slots, and values.

An end-to-end architecture based on the pointer network can effectively extract unknown slot values while achieving state-of-the-art accuracy on the standard dialogue state tracking challenge 2 benchmark.
The proposed deep utterance aggregation model outperforms the state-of-the-art methods on multi-turn conversation benchmarks.
Generative sequence-to-sequence models will effectively incorporate dialogue context and generate meaningful and diverse responses.

The joint dialogue-level satisfaction estimation model achieved up to an absolute 27% improvement in linear correlation performance over baseline deep neural net and benchmark Gradient boosting regression models.
The deep sequential model for parsing discourse dependency structures of multi-party dialogues outperforms all the state-of-the-art baselines.
Cost-sensitive active learning framework can effectively achieve the same dialogue state tracking performance with significantly less data annotation compared to traditional training approaches.
Sequence-to-sequence generation based data augmentation framework will improve the language understanding module in task-oriented dialogue systems.
Co-attention models which explicitly encode dialogue history outperform models that don’t, achieving state-of-the-art performance.

The dual-learning framework for dialogue state tracking effectively alleviates the reward sparsity problem and performs comparably to systems using fully labeled data, especially when labeled data is limited.
MultiWOZ 2.2 will improve dialogue state tracking by fixing annotation errors and redefining the ontology.
The Meta-Reinforced Multi-Domain State Generator will improve the dialogue state tracking accuracy from 48.79% to 50.91% on the MultiWOZ corpus.
Using multiple spoken language understanding engines in state tracking increases both recall and precision of dialog state tracking.

The Selectively Overwriting Memory for Dialogue State Tracking model achieves state-of-the-art joint goal accuracy with 51.72% in MultiWOZ 2.0 and 53.01% in MultiWOZ 2.1 in an open vocabulary-based dialogue state tracking setting.

The deep neural model integrates feature extraction from convolutional neural networks into sequence modeling in recurrent neural networks, optimized by utterance and dialog level attentions, and outperforms the previous state-of-the-art model on this task in a different genre using bidirectional long short-term memory, showing a 13.0+% improvement for longer dialogs.
An online adaptative strategy can significantly improve the performance of the spoken language understanding module on the second Dialog State Tracking Challenge datasets.
The Recurrent Embedding Dialogue Policy (REDP) significantly outperforms a baseline LSTM classifier on dialogue tasks.
The seq2seq model tailored for dialogue act classification achieves an unmatched accuracy score of 85% on SwDA and a state-of-the-art accuracy score of 91.6% on MRDA.
Pre-trained Seq2Seq model outperforms other data augmentation methods in a low-resource setting.
The glimpse-model produces longer and higher quality conversation responses compared to baseline sequence-to-sequence models.
Modeling the reference resolution as a dialogue context-aware user query reformulation task will significantly improve dialogue state tracking in multi-domain scenarios.
The generic dialogue state tracker will achieve comparable performance in hypothesis accuracy to machine learning based systems.
A simple Long Short-Term Memory sequential discourse parser takes advantage of a multi-view and multi-task framework with 12-15% error reductions over the baseline.
The semantic-enhanced Gaussian mixture model (SEG) achieves competitive results on three real task-oriented dialogue datasets in two languages for unknown intent detection.
Hierarchical variational memory network will significantly improve dialogue generation performance compared to state-of-the-art baselines.
The parallel Seq2Seq network with coverage attention will alleviate the overlapping phrase problem in keyphrase generation.
Incorporating structured attention layers into a Variational Recurrent Neural Network model with discrete latent states will enable the model to learn dialogue structure in an unsupervised fashion.

given_idea: Equipping the pre-trained language model-based extractor with a knowledge-guided prompt can effectively alleviate concept bias and improve the performance of concept extraction models.

top_k.sentence: Chain-of-thought prompts will improve performance in metaphor understanding tasks for large language models.
Transformer-based models will improve clinical concept extraction performance compared to traditional models.
An iterative context-aware prompter will effectively elicit relevant knowledge from pre-trained language models for multi-step inference.
Prompt transfer is promising for improving prompt tuning in natural language processing.
PromptSource addresses the emergent challenges in using natural language prompts to train and query language models.
Few-shot instruction-based method for prompting pre-trained language models will detect different types of fine-grained biases with similar and sometimes superior accuracy to fine-tuned models.
Reporting bias poses a challenge for knowledge extraction from text.
Replacing document retrievers with large language model generators will improve performance on knowledge-intensive tasks.
Maximizing mutual information between the induced label and its context will improve part-of-speech induction performance.
Knowledge-aware Prompt-tuning approach with synergistic optimization (KnowPrompt) will effectively incorporate knowledge among relation labels into prompt-tuning for relation extraction.
Using unsupervised word representations as extra word features will improve the accuracy of supervised natural language processing systems.
Label prompt dropout will lead to improved class representations and significantly better results on the few-shot relation extraction task.
Jointly modelling sentence structures and utilizing knowledge from pretraining models will improve performance on the Winograd Schema Challenge.
The joint model for language interpretation and concept learning yields a 30% relative improvement in F1 score over competitive methods in the low data regime.
Injecting syntactic biases into BERT pretraining through knowledge distillation improves performance on structured prediction tasks.
The bag-of-concepts approach will improve relation extraction performance in a narrow knowledge domain with limited data compared to traditional methods.
Combining lexical, syntactic, and semantic features with maximum entropy models will improve information extraction performance.

An efficient method for retrieving prompts for in-context learning using annotated data and a language model will substantially outperform prior work and multiple baselines across sequence-to-sequence tasks.
Incorporating dependency tree information into pre-trained transformer models will improve performance on information extraction tasks.
Explainable artificial intelligence methods can help in combating biases in natural language processing models.
ConceptX enables the discovery and annotation of latent concepts in pre-trained Language Models, facilitating the identification of biases in these models.
Concurrent modeling of multiple biases in the training data will improve generalization performance on both in-domain and out-of-domain datasets in extractive question answering.
Including explanations in the prompts for large language models yields small to moderate accuracy improvements over standard few-shot learning.
Retrieval-augmented generation models will outperform parametric sequence-to-sequence models and task-specific retrieve-and-extract architectures on knowledge-intensive NLP tasks.
Semantic role labeling techniques will improve the performance of Open Information Extraction compared to existing shallow syntactic methods.
Incorporating segment information into neural relation extraction will improve the performance of relation extraction models.

A prompting-based approach to generate labelled training data for intent classification with off-the-shelf language models will significantly boost the performance of intent classifiers when intents in consideration are sufficiently distinct from each other.
Developing and refining prompt-based strategies will significantly enhance the performance of large language models for clinical named entity recognition tasks.
Knowledge-enhanced models improve performance on both discriminative and generative tasks for interpreting figurative language.
Prompt distribution learning will effectively adapt a pre-trained vision-language model to downstream recognition tasks by learning low-bias prompts and capturing the distribution of diverse prompts.
Integrating information from a retrieval system with a pre-trained language model in a purely unsupervised way dramatically improves performance.
Microsoft Concept Graph improves understanding of human languages through concept tagging APIs and semantic extraction.
A novel concept transfer learning approach will improve adaptive language understanding by addressing data sparsity through hierarchical semantic representation.
Word embeddings can add value to the problem of unsupervised part-of-speech induction.
Integrating distributed representations with terminology extraction will improve the specificity assessment of words in the target corpus.
A template-based input representation considerably improves the model’s generalization capability in data-to-text generation.
Progressive self-training with a discriminator will significantly improve aspect term extraction performance compared to conventional self-training methods.
Generative and discriminative algorithms for spoken language understanding will improve concept accuracy, generalization, and robustness to annotation ambiguities compared to previous approaches.
Automatically generated prompts are a viable parameter-free alternative to existing probing methods for eliciting knowledge from masked language models.
Equipping pre-trained language models with structured knowledge via learning from raw text will improve performance on benchmark datasets.
Using a conditional language model for pretraining with unlabeled dialog data will improve the performance of sentiment classifiers compared to state-of-the-art strategies.
Automatically discovering better prompts can improve the accuracy of estimating the knowledge contained in language models from 31.1% to 39.6%.

A novel method for detecting potential false negative training examples using a knowledge inference method improves the performance of relation extraction systems trained using distantly supervised data.
The pre-trained CLIN-X language models outperform other pre-trained transformer models for clinical concept extraction tasks.
Neural extractive summarization systems could benefit from different types of model architectures, transferable knowledge, and learning schemas.
The proposed relation extractor architecture exploits the implicit knowledge of a language model through a question-answering approach.
Pretrained language models can induce natural language rules from natural language facts.
Prompting for biomedical relation extraction will improve performance on the ChemProt dataset compared to regular fine-tuning.
Self-training event extraction systems will improve performance by bootstrapping additional training data from paraphrase clusters.
Pre-trained language model based active learning approach for sentence matching can achieve greater accuracy with fewer labeled training instances.

given_idea: This large-scale dataset is specifically designed to facilitate the development of AI systems capable of understanding and generating human-like text for various applications.
original: MATINF is the first jointly labeled large-scale dataset for classification, question answering and summarization.

top_k.sentence: The introduction of two huge datasets for text summarization and keyword generation will provide significant resources for improving the performance of automated text generation methods.
The BIGPATENT dataset will enable the development of systems that can understand an article’s global content structure and produce abstractive summaries with a high compression ratio.
A scheme for effectively performing the full, complex task with crowd annotators will allow the collection of large scale annotated datasets for natural language understanding tasks.
The dataset proposed in this challenge will be an important milestone towards more robust and general models that can exploit scene text to achieve holistic image understanding.
The corpus generation framework will create challenging datasets for natural language generation models that can handle complex interactions during micro-planning.
A large dataset extracted from the Stack Exchange Data Explorer website can be used for training neural natural language interfaces for databases.
The presented dataset opens new challenges for research in question answering and reasoning across multiple modalities.
WikiHow dataset will provide a diverse and high-quality resource for training abstractive summarization models.
The multilingual dataset will enable new research directions for the text summarization community.
The Schema-Guided Dialogue dataset will provide a challenging testbed for tasks including language understanding, slot filling, dialogue state tracking, and response generation.
Machine learning approaches to natural-language processing may provide specific scientific insights into the nature of human language.
The ToTTo dataset can serve as a useful research benchmark for high-precision conditional text generation.
The curated dataset provides an excellent signal for learning vector representations of sentence meaning.
The proposed dataset can be used to address various OCR and parsing tasks.
The algorithm constructs large-scale corpora automatically, and its performance is close to that of humans.
Kernels for various natural language structures will allow rich, high dimensional representations of these structures.
The large-scale human-created cloze test dataset CLOTH requires a deeper language understanding and a wider attention span than previously automatically-generated cloze datasets.
The dataset will help train and evaluate keyword spotting systems more effectively than conventional datasets used for automatic speech recognition of full sentences.
Current natural language processing technology can be used to automatically produce plot unit representations for narrative text.
The corpus enables future research on the recognition of emotions and associated entities in text.
Automatically generating a large heterogeneous multilingual multi-document summarization corpus will facilitate the development of machine learning methods for multi-document summarization.
KorQuAD1.0 will serve as a large-scale Korean dataset for extractive machine reading comprehension tasks, facilitating the development of multilingual natural language processing research.
Sampling strategies that result in more human-like text create distributional differences from human-written text that make detection easy for automatic discriminators.
The data set is used to train prediction models for valence and arousal from text which achieve high predictive accuracy.

Leveraging human language processing data can increase the performance of natural language processing models and enhance understanding of the differences between human and machine language processing.
A corpus-based system for automatically creating test datasets will cover all degrees of relatedness and all types of lexical-semantic relations.

The ManyNames dataset provides a rich resource for studying object naming phenomena, showing a high level of agreement in naming and a greater number of name types associated with objects compared to existing corpora.
The entity-based representation is well-suited for ranking-based generation and text classification tasks.

The obtained dataset can be used to build text-based classifiers able to predict the overall quality of a counseling conversation and provide insights into the linguistic differences between low-quality and high-quality counseling.
Creating a large-scale data set for idiom translation will facilitate better performance in Neural Machine Translation systems.
Training high quality word representations for 157 languages on large corpora will lead to strong performance in natural language processing tasks.
COCO-Text dataset will advance state-of-the-art in text detection and recognition in natural images.
Information density and quality estimation features will effectively detect and classify human translated texts.

The dataset forms the basis of a shared task on commonsense and script knowledge organized at SemEval 2018 and provides challenging test cases for the broader natural language understanding community.
IMAGENE, an instructional text generation system, will effectively express precondition relations based on the communicative context identified through knowledge intensive corpus analysis.
A well-structured text corpus can directly influence the performance of various Natural Language Processing applications.
Releasing a new large dataset will help develop more accurate algorithms for sequential sentence classification in medical abstracts.
The UAM CorpusTool will facilitate human and semi-automatic annotation of text and images.

The modular, pipeline-based approach for generating natural language descriptions from structured data will exhibit greater scalability and adaptability compared to existing end-to-end statistical and neural architectures.
Creating large-scale datasets that focus on points and paths in the real world will support advances in spatial language understanding.
Coherent story generation from independent descriptions can be effectively approached through Statistical Machine Translation and Deep Learning methods.
TextComplexityDE dataset will facilitate the development of text-complexity predictor models and automatic text simplification in the German language.
Data2Text Studio improves interactivity and interpretability of generated text through automated text generation from structured data.
The Ubuntu Dialogue Corpus provides a unique resource for research into building dialogue managers based on neural language models that can make use of large amounts of unlabeled data.
JuICe dataset aids in code generation conditioned on natural language history and is challenging for current systems.
The algorithm will effectively project target word embeddings to high-dimensional sparse vectors and generate human-readable textual definitions, enabling direct interpretation of word embeddings.
The unified model based on the human-like reading strategy is effective and yields state-of-the-art results on the WikiQA dataset.
RankGAN will generate high-quality language descriptions by analyzing and ranking a collection of human-written and machine-written sentences.
The present success of representation learning approaches trained on large text corpora can be deeply enriched from the parallel tradition of research on the contextual and social nature of language.
Random projections of an input text have enough representational power to encode the hierarchical structure of sentences and semantics of documents.

given_idea: Static and contextualized models can adequately represent different lexical-semantic relations, such as homonymy and synonymy, but monolingual models based on Transformers fail at representing words with different senses when occurring in similar sentences.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: Despite the recent successes of large language models on tasks aimed to assess commonsense knowledge, these models do not greatly outperform simple word-level models based on pre-trained word embeddings.
Contextualised word representations fail to consistently model inferences that require reasoning with tense and aspect.
Powerful contextualized word representations deal less well with the subtle in-context similarity relationships needed for substitution compared to models trained with this objective in mind.
Current neural network models are not able to generalize in capturing the semantics of natural language inference, but seem to be overfitting to the specific dataset.
Language models can adapt to different schemas defined by multiple commonsense knowledge graphs but fail to reuse the knowledge to generalize to new relations.

Existing models trained on language modeling and translation produce strong representations for syntactic phenomena, but only offer comparably small improvements on semantic tasks over a non-contextual baseline.
The representations learned by the InferSent model fail to capture the needed compositionality.
Multimodal image-language transformers fail more in situations that require verb understanding compared to other parts of speech.
Context-aware machine translation models often fail to accurately disambiguate pronouns and polysemous words that require context for resolution.
Multilingual BERT creates multilingual representations, but these representations exhibit systematic deficiencies affecting certain language pairs.
The use of human references as ground truth for cross-language word embeddings is not proper unless one does not understand how native speakers process semantics in their cognition.
Pre-trained multilingual models do not necessarily guarantee high-quality representations on code-switching, while using meta-embeddings achieves similar results with significantly fewer parameters.
BERT’s processing of blends does not fully access the component meanings, leaving their contextual representations semantically impoverished.
A variety of common architectures for natural language inference inevitably fail to encode crucial information; only a model with forced lexical alignments avoids this damaging information loss.

Neural models generalize well to unseen words in tests on five languages but provide no clear benefit over the naïve baseline for downstream part-of-speech tagging of an English historical collection.
BERT does not fully live up to the natural expectations for a semantic vector space.
Distributional semantic models need to be extended to account for language use in a discourse or dialogue context.
A thorough experimental study on Unimer reveals that neural semantic parsing approaches exhibit notably different performance when they are trained to generate different meaning representations.

Transformer-based plausibility models are markedly inconsistent across the conceptual classes of a lexical hierarchy, and a simple post-hoc method of forcing model consistency improves correlation with human plausibility judgements.
Contextual Language Model (BERT) encodes sentence-level properties even within single-word embeddings, obtaining comparable or even superior results than those obtained with sentence representations.

Combining the outputs of graph-based and transition-based dependency parsers no longer leads to increased accuracy due to the mitigation of the chief weakness of graph-based models with deep contextualized word representations.

Exposing language models to a related language does not always increase grammatical knowledge in the target language, and optimal conditions for lexical-semantic transfer may not be optimal for syntactic transfer.
The joint semantic language model can generate better semantic sequences than models that operate on the word level.

The overall low reliability observed in neural word embeddings casts doubt on the suitability of word neighborhoods in embedding spaces for qualitative conclusions on synchronic and diachronic lexico-semantic matters.
Contextualized word embeddings capture human-like distinctions between English word senses, such as polysemy and homonymy.
Capturing linguistic semantics requires more sophisticated models.

Current natural language inference models do not perform consistently well on transitivity inference tasks, suggesting that they lack the generalization capacity for drawing composite inferences from provided training examples.
Most current neural network models are not able to generalize well in the task of natural language inference.

The commonsense ability of GPT, BERT, XLNet, and RoBERTa is promoted by language modeling objectives, bi-directional context, and larger training sets, but current models perform poorly on tasks requiring more inference steps.
Pre-trained language models can identify analogies to a certain extent, but struggle with abstract and complex relations.
Using multilingual contextual word representations in very low-resource settings will yield better results than using cross-lingual static embeddings.
Bilingual word embeddings fail on the task of bilingual lexicon induction for rare and domain-specific words, and new techniques can improve their performance.
Vector space models of semantics will address the limitations of computers in understanding human language.
Different language models exhibit qualitatively different reasoning abilities, and language models do not reason in an abstract manner but are context-dependent.
Models trained on edits encode different aspects of semantics and discourse than models trained on raw text.
The performance of cross-lingual word embedding models largely depends on the task at hand and optimizing these models for bilingual lexicon induction may hurt downstream performance.
Neural word prediction models can distinguish grammatical from ungrammatical sentences with high accuracy, but multilingual models generally underperform monolingual models.
Long contexts, but not their detailed syntactic and propositional content, are important for the low perplexity of current transformer language models.
BERT embeddings do not manage to find robust ways to exploit in lexical semantic change detection.

Using dictionary definitions as a means of bridging the gap between lexical and phrasal semantics will enable neural language embedding models to effectively learn representations of phrases and sentences.
State-of-the-art models perform poorly on new semantic fragments, but with fine-tuning, a BERT-based model can master these fragments while retaining performance on established benchmarks.
Vector-space models of semantic representation have common misconceptions that arise from incomplete descriptions, outdated arguments, and unclear distinctions between theory and implementation.
An LSTM language model deals with lexical ambiguity in English by representing both lexical and contextual information to a large extent, but there is room for improvement for contextual information.
Future progress in distributional semantic models will require balancing the often conflicting demands of linguistic expressiveness and computational tractability.
Multilingual multimodal models do not treat languages equally, exhibiting severe multilingual accuracy disparity and biases across languages.
Eliminative connectionist models cannot account for how we extend universals to arbitrary items.
Different models differ substantially in their ability to reflect certain kinds of regularities in sentence vector representations.
Two recently proposed neural models that perform well on formal texts are limited in their performance when applied to social media data.
Phrase representation in deep transformer models relies heavily on word content, with little evidence of nuanced composition.
Multilingual language models exhibit negligible performance decreases compared to their monolingual counterparts when the languages are adequately represented in the multilingual model’s vocabulary.

given_idea: FrugalScore retains 96.8% of the performance, runs 24 times faster, and has 35 times less parameters than the original metrics.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: PROSPERA will substantially outperform prior results in terms of recall, with the same precision, while having low run-times.
The Quickhull algorithm runs faster when the input contains nonextreme points and uses less memory compared to traditional algorithms.
Block pruning approach will yield a pruned model that is 2.4x faster and 74% smaller with a 1% drop on F1 compared to existing models.
The proposed method of maintaining per-row and per-column sums of moving averages for parameter updates will produce similar results to the baseline while using less memory.

The resulting recurrent neural network model can be smaller, faster during training and testing, and more accurate than the basic one.
CRAIG achieves a speedup that is inversely proportional to the size of the subset while performing on par with the full dataset.

The simplified model CodeMatcher outperforms DeepCS by 97% in terms of Mean Reciprocal Rank and is over 66 times faster than DeepCS.

8-bit optimizers will maintain 32-bit performance with a small fraction of the memory footprint on a range of tasks.

State-of-the-art models achieve 73.8% accuracy on the WIQA dataset, which is well below the human performance of 96.3%.
Optimization strategies allow short-circuiting score computations in additive learning systems, speeding up the score computations by more than four times with almost no loss in result quality.
FastSum will achieve comparable accuracy to the best systems while maintaining fast processing times by relying on a minimal set of features.
The ReCo-RL model achieves better performance than state-of-the-art baselines on both traditional metrics and the proposed new criteria.

FlyingSquid achieves the same or higher quality compared to previous approaches without the need to tune a stochastic gradient descent procedure and recovers model parameters 170 times faster on average.
A memory augmented model with only 12 layers outperforms a baseline transformer model with 24 layers, while being twice faster at inference time.

RELNOUN 2.2 obtains 3.5 times yield with over 15 point improvement in precision compared to RELNOUN 1.1.
DyNet's speeds are faster than or comparable with static declaration toolkits, and significantly faster than Chainer.
GraphOrder algorithm achieves as high as 38.4% absolute improvement in F1 over a random baseline.
