Optimizer performance varies greatly across tasks.
Adaptive computation with global prioritisation improves over several strong static and adaptive methods, leading to a 4.3x reduction in computation while retaining 95% performance of the full model.
EfficientNetV2 models train much faster than state-of-the-art models while being up to 6.8x smaller.
Pruning BERT, RoBERTa, and XLNet models by up to 40% maintains up to 98% of their original performance.
The Cascade Transformer reduces computation by 37% with almost no impact on accuracy compared to a state-of-the-art transformer model.
The word-dependent N-best algorithm is computationally much less expensive than the exact algorithm while resulting in the same accuracy.
Using only 20% of the trigger-annotated sentences results in comparable performance as using 70% of conventional annotated sentences.
RoBERTa achieves a 31% improvement in F1-score and a 41% improvement in Mean Reciprocal Rank over the traditional SVM.
Efficient Conformer architecture achieves better performances with faster training and decoding compared to the Conformer architecture within the same computing budget.

Sockeye 2 will result in faster training and inference, higher automatic metric scores, and a shorter path from research to production compared to previous versions of the Sockeye neural machine translation toolkit.
Best–worst scaling produces significantly more reliable results than the rating scale with the same total number of annotations.
SQLite is now up to 4.2X faster on SSB as a result of our optimizations.
The best combination of features will outperform the baseline from data intensive approaches by 4% for comparison and 16% for contingency.
The method has a relative improvement of at least 5.62% compared to the state-of-the-art on multiple metrics on the recommendation task.
LOVE (LanczOs Variance Estimates) computes covariances up to 2,000 times faster and draws samples 18,000 times faster than existing methods, all without sacrificing accuracy.
The ExStream algorithm for memory efficient rehearsal will perform well using far less memory and computation compared to full rehearsal in streaming learning settings.
FedProx demonstrates significantly more stable and accurate convergence behavior relative to FedAvg, improving absolute test accuracy by 22% on average.
The proposed modifications allow for models that are 24x faster on CPU and 14x faster on GPU at comparable or higher BLEU values.
The proposed hyperparameter tuning algorithm finds good hyperparameter settings nearly an order of magnitude faster than random search.
Joey NMT achieves performance comparable to more complex toolkits on standard benchmarks.

The architectural method based on residual adapters and a simple replay-based strategy perform better compared to other continuous learning techniques and only slightly worse than the multitask learning upper bound while being 20X faster in learning new domains.
The proposed parser gives comparable accuracies to the state-of-the-art chart parsers and is over an order of magnitude faster than the fastest chart parser.
The relative performance differences between various model architectures often shrinks and sometimes even reverses when compared to prior results.
Training a pruning policy under an objective that measures end-to-end performance leads to better parsers which are more accurate for a given runtime.
DeLighT delivers similar or better performance than transformer-based models with significantly fewer parameters.
Ensembles of system combinations obtained best scores of 0.476, 0.702, and 88.7%, outperforming individual systems.
Canine outperforms a comparable mBert model by 5.7 F1 on TyDi QA, despite having fewer model parameters.

given_idea: The proposed method achieves significant improvements in expanding course concepts in MOOCs compared to existing methods.
original: NA

top_k.sentence: The proposed method significantly outperforms the state-of-the-art lifelong learning approaches.
The proposed method results in a substantial boost in performance on the event ordering task compared to previous approaches.

The trained student ResNet exhibited better performance in terms of classification accuracy and fast learning than the existing teacher-student framework-based knowledge transfer methods considered in this study.
The proposed solution is significantly more effective than applying state-of-the-art set expansion or holistic ranking techniques.
Detailed access trajectories contain rich information about students' learning behaviors and facilitate MOOC learning analyses.
The structured content preserving model will achieve significant improvement in content preservation and style transfer compared to existing approaches.
The proposed method yields substantial improvements in translation quality and speed over each base system.
The proposed method can achieve better or competitive performance on the out-of-vocabulary problem compared with existing state-of-the-art methods.
The proposed method significantly outperforms existing approaches in image classification experiments by a large margin.
The sense-topic model for word sense induction will achieve significant improvements over the previous state-of-the-art methods.
The proposed method produces curricula customized for each agent that improve performance relative to learning from scratch or using a different agent’s curriculum.
The tagging based methods are better than most of the existing pipelined and joint learning methods.
The proposed pretraining objectives result in better performance, better convergence, models that are less data hungry, and better domain generalizability.
The proposed method leads to promising improvements on task success rate and total task reward compared to supervised training and single-agent reinforcement learning training baseline models.
The model for automatically clustering questions into user intents will show an impressive improvement over previous methods for two languages/domains.

Transferring knowledge from previous optimizations to new tasks will significantly speed up the optimization process compared to the standard single-task approach.

The method for constructing an image embedding system from any existing n-way image classifier and a semantic word embedding model outperforms state of the art methods on the ImageNet zero-shot learning task.
Rationale-augmented ensembles will achieve more accurate and interpretable results than existing prompting approaches.

Knowledge Embedding based Graph Convolutional Network (KE-GCN) offers an elegant unification of several well-known Graph Convolutional Network methods as specific cases, with a new perspective of graph convolution.
DeepType outperforms all existing solutions for Entity Linking by integrating symbolic information into the reasoning process of a neural network.
